wholefestival_description: 2THEMAX is a DJ and one of the founders of Korgy, a collective at the heart of Helsinki’s growing queer club scene. Inspired by dancefloors and queer nightlife across Europe, his approach to DJing is all about freedom, self-expression, and keeping it FUN. His sets blend high-energy techno, euphoric trance, and thick progressive house, with a focus on mood & movement. Never tied to one sound, 2THEMAX follows the energy of the room—open, playful, and always surprising. Recent gigs outside of Finland have included DAS in Copenhagen KitKat Club & RSO in Berlin with the Korgy crew for Gegen and PRNCPTL, and London’s FOLD for Boudica. Through his work with Korgy, he’s helping shape a new wave of inclusive nightlife in Finland, building spaces where music, identity, and joy come together. Photographer: Tatu Rouvinen

wholefestival_description: 3RODA FULL BASS is an Indonesian DJ trio who serves up a delirious mix of a messy, joyful mix of Indonesian club sounds like funkot, bass beton, Indo Bounce, and jungle dutch. Named after silly tracks about mosquitos, pests and general chaos, 3RODA FULL BASS is here to turn up the heat, get the floor bouncing, and keep the night itching to dance.

wholefestival_description: aantz is the artist personality of Argentina born, Berlin based composer, live performer & multihuman Emme Moises. As a curator of experiences in this dimension, aantz performs live in sync with her machines. Her live sets evolve around the frontiers of ambient & experimental polybeats, with a focus on minimalism and dashes of deep sci-fi soundscapes.\n\nPhoto by Gabriel Strobel

wholefestival_description: Berlin-based DJ and Cuddles co-founder Aaron Blau moves dance floors with instinct, warmth, and a storyteller’s touch. A dedicated selector, he seamlessly blends rare cuts, overlooked gems, and fresh contemporary music into cohesive, emotionally driven sets. Known for his instinctive energy and a subtle sense of nostalgia, he crafts journeys that are eclectic yet cohesive, queer without cliché, and always sexy. He has appeared at Pornceptual, Gegen, Power Dance Club, SchwuZ and K4 and maintains a residency at Milan’s Vitamina, with mixes contributed to Radiant Records, The Mudd Show, and Smut Press. Photographer: Thomás Eyzaguierre

ACIDNENA is an Argentine DJ based in Barcelona, known for her high-energy, genre-fluid sets that move between techno, trance, hard groove, Latin underground, and new rave. A resident at Nitsa Club and part of the queer collective LATINEO, she has become a key figure within the local FLINTA* and migrant scenes. Her trajectory spans clubs, festivals, and underground spaces across Spain and South America, where she delivers powerful, identity driven performances. Photographer: Laradude

Adam Munnings is a Tasmanian-born, Berlin-based DJ and creative director known for shaping vibrant queer dance floors. His sound blends house, disco, breakbeat, techno and pop-infused edits, delivering energetic sets built for joy, movement and collective release. Adam began DJing in Tokyo and later lived in New York before settling in Berlin, where he co-founded the influential queer rave Lunchbox Candy. After four years of building its community and global reputation, he now serves as co-founder, creative director and resident DJ of tracey, a new queer-led event series pushing the boundaries of nightlife culture. Photographer: Rod Saad
soundcloud_description: Tasmanian born, Berlin based DJ & film maker. Co-founder, creative director & resident Dj at tracey. Co-founder & resident DJ at Lunchbox Candy 2021 - 2025.

wholefestival_description: ADIRA is a queer Arabic-pop club night and collective. Founded in 2023 by Lebanese artist Hassandra and Syrian-German DJ xanax_attax, ADIRA is a self-organized club night series that challenges orientalist stereotypes and embraces the diversity of queer Arab* identities. Our mission is to revive the nostalgia of 80’s, 90’s, and early 00’s Arabic-pop music, paying homage to iconic divas like Nawal Al Zoghbi, Haifa Wehbe, and Assala, alongside the unforgettable one-hit wonders from Melody Hits.

wholefestival_description: Alex Hawthorn (b. 1986) [they/them] is an artist whose work flows between performance, installation, object-making, and sound-making. Their live performances build emotion through layered soundscapes that slowly morph over time using archival audio, homemade instruments, and live electronics.These are long, evolving sets that can unfold over multiple hours.

Alírio is a Brazilian DJ and producer, a dynamic and ever-evolving force in the electronic music scene. Her sound moves fluidly across genres, delivering high-energy sets infused with hypnotic rhythms and a raw, sensual edge. With a strong focus on Latin American productions, she explores bass-driven and percussive elements in vibrant and innovative ways. Alírio is a resident of the renowned Mamba Negra collective (São Paulo) and a resident DJ at SEXTOU (London). She has also performed at key events across Brazil, including Gop Tun Festival (São Paulo) and Kode (Rio de Janeiro). Since 2024, she has performed internationally at venues and festivals including NYC (Basement, Nowadays), Germany (Whole Festival, Fusion Festival, Tresor, Herrensauna, Pornceptual), Barcelona (Sala Apolo, Razzmatazz), Lisbon (Planeta Manas, Outra Cena), London (Club Stamina), Netherlands (Draaimolen, Club Raum), Brussels (Horst), Bogotá (Video Club), and Buenos Aires (Deseo, 999). She is also the co-founder of Tandera Records, a multidisciplinary label highlighting boundary-pushing electronic music by some of today’s most inventive artists from Brazil and beyond. Photographer: ivimaigabugrimenko

Ambivalentina (she/her) is a queer dancer, aerial hoop artist and performer with kazakh-russian roots. Based in Vienna, she has performed at various festivals and events in Austria, Germany, Spain, Croatia and Italy, working in and with multidisciplinary art collectives and dance companies. The movement artist explores the intersection of politically engaged performance practices and movement. Currently she is also working as a freelance actress appearing in shortfilms and wishes to integrate the topic of intersectional feminism into future art and movement projects. Photographer: valerie lola

wholefestival_description: ANTI-MASS is a queer collective from Kampala, Uganda — founded in 2018 by Nsasi and Authentically Plastic as an act of joy, defiance and very loud music. They make dancefloor-wrecking electronic music rooted in East African rhythms, touring relentlessly across Europe, the Americas and Asia. For their upcoming Whole Festival performance, they're joined by Seoul-based artist NET-GALA.
soundcloud_description: booking@anti-mass.com

wholefestival_description: Armana Khan is a DJ, model, and activist shaking up stages and igniting dance floors across the globe. She fuses her desi roots with global beats, aiming to create unity and peace through sound. Her sets aren't just club music - they're cultural lessons, seamlessly blending tracks from around the world with the hope that music will bring us together whether we understand the lyrics or have ever heard the beat before.\n\nSHE TRULY BELIEVES WORLD MUSIC CAN BRING WORLD PEACE
soundcloud_description:
Armana Khan is a DJ, model, and activist shaking up stages and igniting dance floors across the globe. She fuses her Desi roots with global rhythms. Her sets are not just music; they are cultural bridges, mixing tabla with techno and bhangra with afrobeat to bring the world together through sound. She says music is a universal language, and that it can teach us about one another and bring peace. Her mission is to turn dance floors into spaces of connection and harmony. World music for world peace is her vision and her vibe.
email for bookings: bookings@discono.club

wholefestival_description: Authentically Plastic is a boundary-splintering artist and DJ from Kampala, Uganda. From 2018–2023, they co-founded and ran ANTI-MASS, a roving, riotous club night that carved out space for queer expression in Kampala. This commitment to disruption and sonic liberation has shaped their accelerated, improvisational style—full of layered intensity and unexpected turns. Their acclaimed debut album Raw Space (2022) distilled this volatile energy into a body of work celebrated for its prismatic unpredictability and urgent, tactile sound. In their DJ sets—at once dark, playful, and physically unrelenting—Authentically Plastic uses Techno, Gqom, and Noise as scaffolding for exploring unknown terrains. Their recordedmusic is informed by Northern Uganda’s Acholi electronic rhythms, early Acid/Industrial aesthetics, and Futurist thinking. It’s dance music that resists containment—dirty, greasy, and always on the verge of transformation. Photographer: Ryan Molnar

wholefestival_description: ābnamā, based in Berlin via Tehran, has established herself as an integral part of the city's queer nightlife. She is a resident at the beloved queer powerhouse Body Language and has built a loyal following playing in various European and Asian cities. An obsessive digger, she is heavily influenced by the obscure and the romantic, be it through American midwest oddities, UK and European post-punk tinged trance and acid or gritty and emotionally charged house grooves which she sifts through with a keen ear for the highest camp. \n\nPhoto by @jellyluise

wholefestival_description: Combining their trajectories, life experiences, and references, this project is a black and queer sonic manifesto. It navigates through House, Funk, Disco, Voguebeat, and other rhythms that move the Black and LGBT community, all with the intent of making the dancefloor sweat, dance, and feel free. Mirands is a DJ and one of the creators of BATEKOO, a collective that made its mark on the scene through its black and LGBTQIA+ protagonism. Balera is an essential presence in São Paulo's nightlife, known for sets full of energy, style, and stance. Both use music as an extension of their identities and as a tool for political expression. Together as Balerands, they create a space where sound, body, and attitude meet. A project rooted in lived experience and expression, where every beat carries freedom, pride and belonging.

BAMBI is a South London based DJ and promoter known for her

Reflecting the Turkish word for ‘different’, BASHKKA’s name highlights her one-of-a kind presence. The Munich-born, NYC raised DJ and producer, renowned for her daring and incisive style, captivates audiences while pushing the boundaries of electronic music. From her residencies at HAMAM Nights, Club Raum and Munich’s Blitz Club, to her solid footing as a regular at Berghain’s Panorama Bar, her standout\nperformances at Glastonbury (listed among Resident Advisor’s 5 Key Performances), Dekmantel Festival, Nuits Sonores, London’s Fabric to legendary Queer parties like ADONIS, are just a few examples. Photographer: RICARDA HAEHN

Reflecting the Turkish word for ‘different,’ BASHKKA’s name highlights her one-of-a-kind presence. Her sets, known for their daring and incisive flair, captivate audiences, while her advocacy for the cultural and political evolution of electronic music brings a revitalizing, forward-thinking energy to the scene. From her residencies at Amsterdam's Club RAUM, Munich’s Blitz Club or to her solid footing as a regular at Berghain’s Panorama Bar, BASHKKA’s sound exudes a powerful energy and rhythm that you have to move your body to.

Irreversibly shaped by a decade in New York City—a place she describes as her rebirth—this period has been pivotal to BASHKKA’s creative and personal development. In a city where cultures intersect, diversity thrives, and LGBTQIA+ history is sacred, the impact on her artistry has been profound.

Outside the DJ booth, the Munich-born and raised artist’s resolute integrity fuels her activism and devotion to her community. Her passionate support for SWANA and queer voices, along with her tribute to dance music culture rooted in Black and Brown communities, redefines electronic music’s rebellious spirit for today’s nightlife. With her debut EP "Maktub" claiming the number one spot in 2023 and taking the music scene by storm, BASHKKA has firmly established herself as a trailblazing producer. Her ability to effortlessly fuse bold creativity with technical mastery is showcased in her collaboration with renowned fashion designer Dilara Fındıkoğlu, as well as in her numerous cutting-edge remixes that don’t just resonate—they set the bar!

wholefestival_description: Miro Lopasso, aka Boyca, is a hard-hitting Techno naturalist: as a queer, Swiss-born DJ, producer, and Berlin transplant, Boyca’s convulsing and maximalist productions channel the transformative and liberating dancefloor hypnotism he experienced into relentless Techno totems with industrial undercurrents—strobe-lit, sweat-drenched, and charged with raw physicality. Debuting on Dutch label Mord with the mangled, slow-burning blowout Beneath in 2015, Boyca has since recast his throttling industrial core into a functional, sexy, and floor-driven framework. The result is an enrapturing blend of shuddering low-end and complex groove structures that cycle through frenzied drum machine sequences, crushed loops, and heaving, spaced-out atmospheres. Over time, Boyca has steadily transfigured his thunderous industrialism into a gritty brand of high-energy Techno with hot-blooded joie de vivre, culminating in 2024 with his most uptempo and floor-focused work. A four-track EP on Berlin label ...is serving, with remixes by Rexer & Nymed, landed as a relentless, fast-paced concrete séance—built for the floor and designed with a writhing mass of bodies in mind. 2025 marked another leap forward with The Necessary Substance (...is serving; June 2025), where Boyca carved out his own pocket of queer club-space—where distortion meets devotion. Across three uncompromising tracks, Boyca channeled raw intensity and hypnotic seduction. With a constant drive to reinvent and push further, Boyca continues to deliver raw and honest dancefloor superlatives anchored in a grinding and granular Hardgroove framework—club music as ritual, release, and transformation.
soundcloud_description:
Miro Lopasso, aka Boyca, is a hard-hitting Techno naturalist: as a queer, Swiss-born DJ, producer, and Berlin transplant, Boyca’s convulsing and maximalist productions channel the transformative and liberating dancefloor hypnotism he experienced into relentless Techno totems with industrial undercurrents—strobe-lit, sweat-drenched, and charged with raw physicality.
Debuting on Dutch label Mord with the mangled, slow-burning blowout Beneath in 2015, Boyca has since recast his throttling industrial core into a functional, sexy, and floor-driven framework. The result is an enrapturing blend of shuddering low-end and complex groove structures that cycle through frenzied drum machine sequences, crushed loops, and heaving, spaced-out atmospheres. Ass It! (Creaked; 2020), The Encounter from Galactic Outposts (Creaked; 2022), Modified Humans (LPS; 2021), Nediar (LPS; 2021), and Remote Controlled (LPS; 2022)—with heavyweight remixers Charlton, Roseen, Stanislav Tolkachev, and Rrose—followed in quick succession, sharpening Boyca’s vision of oscillating sound design, pounding groove structures, and infinitely morphing apogees with a dystopian undercurrent.
Over time, Boyca has steadily transfigured his thunderous industrialism into a gritty brand of high-energy Techno with hot-blooded joie de vivre, culminating in 2024 with his most uptempo and floor-focused work. A four-track EP on Berlin label …is serving, with remixes by Rexer & Nymed, landed as a relentless, fast-paced concrete séance—built for the floor and designed with a writhing mass of bodies in mind.
2025 marked another leap forward with The Necessary Substance (…is serving; June 2025), the label’s ninth release, where Boyca carved out his own pocket of queer club-space—where distortion meets devotion. Across three uncompromising tracks, Boyca channeled raw intensity and hypnotic seduction: Scream_ unraveled in urgent vocals and tightly coiled percussion; The Necessary Substance simmered with eerie minimal tension and fragmented samples; and Deep Thxxxt_ delivered a direct, bass-heavy weapon built for smoke-filled basements. The subsequent Necessary Substance Remixes (September 2025) expanded the release into new territories, reaffirming Boyca as a distinct voice in contemporary techno—fierce, sensual, and unafraid to push boundaries.
With a constant drive to reinvent and push further, Boyca continues to deliver raw and honest dancefloor superlatives anchored in a grinding and granular Hardgroove framework—club music as ritual, release, and transformation.

wholefestival_description: Budino is an Italian DJ and producer based in Berlin since 2012. Every free moment is devoted to digging for new music and discovering new ways to weave her findings together. Her sets unfold like psychedelic tapestries, blending industrial beats, tribal percussion, and richly layered sonic textures. After more than a decade playing some of Berlin’s most iconic queer dance floors — including a residency at the infamous Cocktail d’Amore at the legendary Griessmühle — her deep connection with New York City led her to co-found the label Sonic Dream Recordings. Alongside this, she also runs her own party, Nothing Special, in both Berlin and NYC. Photographer: Alicia Carrera

Budino is an Italian DJ and producer who has been based in Berlin since 2012. Every free moment is devoted to digging for new music and exploring fresh ways to weave her discoveries together. Her sets unfold like psychedelic tapestries, blending industrial beats, tribal percussion, and rich sonic textures.
Over the years, Budino has refined her craft through unique edits and reworks that bring new life and versatility to the rare gems she tirelessly unearths. After a decade playing Berlin’s most iconic queer dance floors, her deep connection with New York City led her to co-found the label Sonic Dream Recordings, while also running her own party, Nothing Special, in both Berlin and NYC.
In addition to her work behind the decks and in the studio, Budino was invited to curate the fourth edition of Love International Festival’s compilation series, The Sound of Love International. 
Budino has performed at some of the world’s most legendary clubs and festivals, including Panorama Bar, Fabric, Club Raum, Basement, Horst Festival, Houghton, Dekmantel Selectors, Making Time, as well as delivering exciting back-to-back sets with Tiga and Ivan Smagghe. She is also co-authoring a book with Bill Brewster, exploring Italian dance music culture and the art of DJing from 1965 to 1995, coming out in 2026.

wholefestival_description: Launching to prominence out of Herrensauna’s infamous underground parties, CEM has forged a singular path as a fierce club DJ unafraid of turning the dancefloor moody. With a preference for UK-tweaked tracks from across the contemporary milieu of more complex club works, nor with a fear of breakbeats or sullen moments, he’s a rare species of DJ that seeds intensity in even the most hesitant of movers. And with a background in queer club culture going back a decade to his hometown of Vienna—side-by-side with a punk upbringing—CEM’s well-versed in the essential metrics of unrelenting after-dark commotion.

wholefestival_description: While the world's online, Chloe Caillet's in the club. Her music and kaleidoscopic DJ sets recall utopic rave histories of the past: dance factories, the Balearic bliss of the '90s; a time when smartphones didn't mediate the clubbing experience. Caillet's music encourages your full presence, your phone deep in your pocket. Within just a few years Caillet, a DJ, producer, multi-instrumentalist, has become one of dance's most vitalizing forces. In 2024 alone she logged tens of thousands of miles playing sets and curating global communities with SMILE, her queer-centered club night. From playing B2Bs with Jamie xx to closing the terrace at Ibiza's Circoloco with Kylie Minogue in attendance, Chloe Caillet has become your favorite pop star's DJ, your favorite DJ's DJ, soon to be your favorite, too. A musician first, Caillet brings her near-perfect pitch to the decks. She mixes in key, thinks in textures and tonalities-fifths, thirds-and instinctively knows the sounds that will mesh together best. Always at least three steps ahead of the track, she creates fine musical details within her sets, setting up perfect harmonies on the fly. Her layering is subtle, but if you lean closer you her years of classical study; her background in improvisational jazz. Photographer: Hector Curly

Club Stamina is a rapidly rising, London-based queer nightlife concept and collective founded by DJ and stylist Mina Galán in early 2024. The project champions trans-femme and female talent, creating a space where bold identities and creative expression take centre stage. Blending high-fashion aesthetics with experimental Latin club sounds — including reggaeton and baile funk — Club Stamina delivers an immersive, high-energy experience that is both culturally driven and unapologetically confident. Known for its sweaty, electric atmospheres and distinctive crowd, the event has quickly gained recognition across London’s nightlife and fashion scenes. Club Stamina has hosted events during Fashion Week and collaborated with brands including Nike and KNWLS, as well as expanding internationally with events in Paris and Colombia. Notable takeovers include the Barbican. More than a party, Club Stamina is a movement — celebrating identity, freedom, and the power of music, fashion, and community.

The cosmic spectres of techno, trance, house, bass and breakbeat. Cora moves with astral intuition—alchemizing her indie acting roots into cinematic sets that radiate with celestial energy, and sear with Sichuan spice. Driven by rolling drums, percussive passages, punchy vocals and transcendent breakdowns, the Chengdu-born DJ weaves between old school trance, progressive house and techno grooves—superimposing dramatic subplots and emotional undertows onto rhythmic narratives, attuned to the kinetic desires of the dancefloor. From her hometown residency at .TAG, Chengdu, to her newfound base in Berlin, Cora's path has been one of perpetual transformation, evolving into an accomplished DJ and producer amid an awakened and accelerating scene. Her debut release, ‘Mountain High’, marked a propulsive blend of progressive trance and her own take on traditional mountain singing, drawing on seven years of classical Italian operatic training. Her latest release, sees a meditative rendition of Buddhist mantra, ‘The Heart Sutra’. Across each of her personal productions, Cora’s confident and ethereal vocal experimentation makes every track distinctly her own. Through her performance, productions, and party series 6G+, Cora blends primal rhythm with otherworldly experience, continuing to embrace a more uplifting and sensual incarnation of her ever-mystical sound—where trance meets transcendence, and fantasy meets physical release.Drawing from her indie film roots, Cora crafts cinematic sets—superimposing dramatic subplots and emotional undertows onto rhythmic narratives, ever-attuned to the kinetic desires of the dancefloor.

wholefestival_description: Photographer: @leonthesinger

Cormac, the Irish born DJ and curator, has risen from an underground secret to a commanding force in modern dance music. His modern take on HI-NRG, Italo, house, techno, and nu-wave captures the pulse of contemporary club culture. A longtime fixture at Berghain / Panorama Bar, he commands dancefloors with an emotional precision and ecstatic energy that have earned him a reputation as one of the most reliable and forward-thinking selectors of his generation. In 2026, his Polari record label and party series will hit the road, touring Berlin, London, and Dublin. He will be joined by a host of DJ talent, artists deeply embedded in and contributing to their local music communities and evolving musical landscapes. Cormac's ability to hold a dance floor in joyful catharsis has made him one of the most trusted selectors in the contemporary scene. His taste displays deep historical awareness, bridging eras of queer club music while pushing its future forward. From Sónar to Bassiani to Glastonbury, Cormac has become a trusted presence on the global festival and club circuit. In 2021 he launched Polari Records, a label dedicated to elevating LGBTQ+ artistry and championing queer creativity on a world stage. Polari has quickly become a trusted platform for both emerging innovators and established pioneers, reflecting Cormac’s broader work as a curator, community advocate, and custodian of queer club heritage. As a producer, he has released on revered labels including Ostgut Ton, Running Back, and his own Polari Records, with remix credits for Lime and the iconic Kim Carnes. His impact extends beyond the booth, with magazine covers in Germany, the U.S., and the UK, and standout mixes for BBC Radio 1’s Essential Mix, Beats in Space, Resident Advisor, and DJ Mag. January 2024 marked the launch of Queerly Beloved, Cormac’s podcast exploring how music shapes the lives of LGBTQIA+ artists and allies guests including Beth Ditto , Peaches, Hercules & Love Affair and Romy. Warm curious, and incisive, he continues to expand his voice as a queer health advocate and cultural archivist—further solidifying his position as one of today’s most compelling and influential figures in electronic music.
soundcloud_description:
Cormac (Polari/Running Back/Ostgut Ton/Correspondant)

Once a hidden gem in the dance music world, Cormac’s reputation as a respected selector has risen . Originally from the North of Ireland, he’s known for his signature mix of HI-NRG, house, techno, and nu-wave—sounds that have made him a fixture and influence on the international electronic music circuit. His acclaimed sets have been notable highlights at clubs and festivals such as Panorama Bar, Sónar, Bassiani, Glastonbury, fabric, WHOLE Festival, and beyond.

A former dancefloor regular at iconic nights like Trade, Nag Nag Nag, and Bugged Out!, Cormac has been DJing for over two decades. His marathon closing sets at Berghain’s Panorama Bar—sometimes running 11 hours or more—have become the stuff of legend, reinforcing his status as one of the scene’s most trusted selectors.

In 2021, he launched Polari Records, a label dedicated to showcasing LGBTQ+ artistry, with releases from both rising talents and established names.

Beyond the booth, Cormac has been featured on magazine covers in Germany, the U.S., and the UK, and has contributed standout mixes to BBC Radio 1’s Essential Mix, Beats in Space, Resident Advisor, and DJ Mag.

In January 2024, he debuted his podcast Cormac’s Queerly Beloved, where he speaks with LGBTQIA+ artists and allies about the role music plays in their lives. As a host, he brings warmth, curiosity, and a deepening commitment to his work as a queer health advocate and queer history archivist.

Cuddles is a vibrant, nonconforming space that blends hedonism with warmth, playfulness, and queer chaos. Messy, horny and heart-warming, it became a space where the freaky, the soft-hearted and the divas find home.\n\nOne word to describe it is eclectic. Cuddles lives in the grey area between binaries, between house and techno, between straight and gay, between cute and bizarre. The true place of madness, delusion and unapologetic fun. wholefestival_description: Formed by the producers GDZ (PB) and RKills (SP), CyberKills is a sonic and visual electronic music project that’s been making waves in the alt scene since 2018. The duo has built an impressive resume of collaborations with artists like Pabllo Vittar, Linn da Quebrada, Urias, Gloria Groove, Jup do Bairro, Carlos do Complexo, Irmãs de Pau, Jaloo, and Chameleo. Blending genres like funk, tribal, techno, and trance, CyberKills has racked up over 25 million streams across all platforms and even landed spots on Spotify’s charts. Their standout tracks include “Descontrolada Remix” with Pabllo Vittar, “Megatron” with Irmãs de Pau, and full-on executive productions like Mia Badgyal’s “Emergência”, Jup do Bairro’s “in.corpo.ração”, and Boombeat’s “Bárbara.” Photographer: Pedro Lacerda

wholefestival_description: DJ, producer and graduate in musical arts. She is one of the most prominent figures of the new generation of Chilean techno. Her name has been established through a versatile, consistent sonic proposal deeply connected to the dancefloor. Over the course of her 6-year career, she has been part of important musical platforms, performing at stages such as Creamfields, Festival Baum, the iconic DAME party, Hör, and through her residency at Techno Youth, among other key spaces in the Latin American circuit. Her ability to adapt to different contexts while maintaining a strong identity positions her as an artist in constant evolution. As a producer, she has released music on labels in Chile, Colombia and Germany, expanding her reach beyond the continent. In parallel, she works as a mastering engineer and composer for film and theatre, demonstrating an artistic sensitivity that transcends the dancefloor. She represents a contemporary and multidisciplinary approach to techno, where technique, emotion and artistic research converge within a single creative body.

wholefestival_description: Emerging from Hanoi, Vietnam, Di Linh has been steadily shaping her presence across the region. Known for her bold, wide-ranging selections and genre-fluid approach to the dance floor, her music feels both intentional and deeply immersive. She holds residencies at Savage, The Observatory, and Equation, and was formerly a resident at Wonderfruit (The Quarry). Seamlessly bringing her unique color across Southeast Asia and beyond, Di Linh draws inspiration from each space she plays, with her sound constantly evolving to pull crowds into a trance while staying true to herself. She crafts mind-bending sets spanning Disco, House, and Techno, guiding the audience’s emotions through her own story.

wholefestival_description: Diva Beirut is a Lebanese drag performer based in Paris joining Adira which is is an Arab*/SWANA club and event series based in Berlin.

Zurich-born, Berlin-based DJ AYA has a keen eye for finessing the vitality, sexiness and punch of any given dancefloor. Their first love, UK bass, captured their imagination over a seven-year stint in London and brought her into electronic music’s binding fold. Bass’ quintessential groove and playfulness have been a lens informing AYA’s gravitational pull towards different genres. From immersive, clean techno kicks to diasporic motifs like grime vocals and reggaeton beats, AYA saw mixing as an opportunity to express the wide-ranging palate they’d long-cultivated as a listener. In only a handful of years, AYA’s career has quickly blown up to whirlwind status with a far-reaching roster of weekly gigs, including standouts like Khidi in Tbilisi, Boiler Room sets in Lisbon and Berlin, and label Live From Earth’s Panorama Bar showcase. AYA, whose intention is always “to bring the queers to dance”, finds great inspiration in the camaraderie of Berlin’s local scene, along with exploring new underground communities in cities like Istanbul and Tokyo. A self-described “hypegirl”, AYA continues to champion their distinctive sound, faithfully meshing their bass-y roots and floor-ready drive. For full AYA immersion, future production is in the works and being perfected. Never slowing down, AYA’s upcoming movements include a thorough Asia tour and further pivots outside continental Europe. Back home, she anticipates a busy spring and summer - most excitedly Europe’s queer weekend of the year, Whole Festival. Like the tracks she plays, AYA commands the desk with a joyous presence and sharp intuitiveness. She’s rightfully become an important player in Berlin’s new wave of DJs channelling the city’s limitless energy and sense of possibility. Photographer: Thomás Eyzaguierre

wholefestival_description: DJ Fuckoff is quickly becoming a star in the constellation of club culture. The DJ, producer and vocalist does things hard, fast and with raw intensity, blending techno, bass, happy hardcore, juke, breakbeat and more. The Berlin-via-New Zealand producer and DJ has always felt alien in some way; like she’s not fully from this Earth. That idea is something she’s relaying through the concept of planet Fucktopia, her own world. Photographer: Lea Taillefer

DJ LOUI’s musical vision combines the most atmospheric Tribal influences with innovative mutant-stomping Techno and surrealist Bass, coordinating time and space in communion with the listeners, that resists trends and styles. His work as curator for projects like @metarave @safadaberlin and @suenalatam festival, is constantly encouraging LATAM artists to make their art visible to a larger audience in Europe, and took him to collaborate with institutions such as @ctmfestival @razzmatazzclubs and @lamachineparis among others. He is constantly in the works to connect scenes and create spaces for alternative club culture. In 2023 he released his first LP called \ Photographer: Nicho Santini

Over the past decade, he has developed a characteristically kinetic sound that blends hard groove, trance, sexy house, and kunty club edits, with emphatic nods to his Kurdish roots. Photographer: Daniel Hjorth

wholefestival_description: DJ Petite is an Irish born, Berlin based DJ whose sets are an immersive exploration of sound from ’90s house to contemporary, booty-bouncing beats. Moving through emotion, rhythm and release, they blend sensual rhythms with raw energy to create dancefloors charged with connection, play and intimacy. Where people can lose themselves and find themselves again. Deeply inspired by the history and roots of queer nightlife, their sound captures the pulse of freedom, desire and the community that defines it. They are a vessel for the community, the music moves through them with a higher purpose of the dancefloor. With residencies at tracey in Berlin and Kinky Sundays in Hamburg, DJ Petite channels the spirit of club kid culture by blurring boundaries, celebrating individuality and reminding us that music isn’t just sound but energy, memory and feeling: a space to heal, to touch, to be free. Photographer: Marina Mónaco

wholefestival_description: Founder of Transit, one of Manila’s most exciting underground parties, known for its collaborations with Elephant and its strong presence in the city’s electronic scene. DUALITY DJ is part of KWIA’s 5-hour takeover, closing the Ambient Stage through to 5am Monday morning.\nPhoto by Cru Camara
soundcloud_description: Duality 「aka Sean Bautista」has a unique take on music that showcases the duplexity of his musical references. His sound is distinctly deep, percussive, and bass-forward, conveying an aversion to rigidity by mixing the old with the new, as well as analogue with digital. These qualities make his sets refined yet complex. Whether he’s transporting the room into mental dimensions or warming up the floor with atmospheric textures, he’s sure to deliver layers of meticulously arranged sonic frequencies.

wholefestival_description: Duo Geboy is an Indonesian queer sensual dance duo, serving hips of the archipelago by storm. Through joget dangdut, they remember, seduce, survive, and celebrate life.\n\nDUO GEBOY is Instant Slurp and Dirandra. They are part of the collective Nusaqueer Diaspora and will be performing along with 3RODA FULL BASS during their set at TRINA.

E.T. is a Kazakh DJ based in Almaty and an emerging key figure in the country’s underground electronic music scene. He is a resident member of the ZVUK collective and co-founder of the MOBILESUBURB record label, which provides a platform for niche artists from his homeland, Kazakhstan. The name E.T. is a play on the Kazakh phrase for “meaty beats,” a fitting reflection of his strong inclination toward dense, hyper-energetic tracks within the realm of leftfield club tools. His sets possess an extraordinary ability to ignite intensity, even in the most reserved dancers. While deeply rooted in Kazakhstan’s electronic music landscape, E.T.’s dynamic performances have also taken him to renowned international parties and festivals, including Herrensauna at Tresor (Germany), WHOLE Festival (Germany), Den Anden Side (Denmark), Office (Armenia), HOT!HOT!HOT! Festival (Norway), Secta (United Arab Emirates), Stihia (Uzbekistan), and PLUR (Kyrgyzstan).

wholefestival_description: Eau de Cologne in collaboration with Bassiani, curates a series of exclusive parties held every two months. Our mission is to create a safe, vibrant, and accepting space for the LGBTQ+ community and allies in Tbilisi, Georgia. Eau de Cologne's parties are more than just events; they are a means to inspire positive change and foster a sense of belonging and empowerment among marginalized communities.

Eau Vive is inspired by sounds that lifts the spirit and gets the ass moving, everything that stirs a sense of nostalgia. A fan of percussion, thumping bass, and electronic beats, she prefers to follow her own flow and the moods that shift with the tides. She’s a DJ who likes to spin stories for the mutants twerking between two chairs. She picks up beats wherever the mood strikes her, but especially from the collections of Black, Afro-Creole, and Latin diaspora clubs. Bubbling, kuduro, logobi, batida, and bouyon rub up against acid, electric, and groovy sounds, which she spices up with vogue beats and queer club influences. A mix firmly rooted in the global south club movement that celebrates the club’s diasporic and underground origins. Photographer: Boolbeny Akabb

Entourage is Atôn, Eau Vive, N0l4.44, and Nyēbra. A queer DJ collective based in Marseille that shares common values, aspirations, and a shared vision of the club and the party scene: community-oriented and honoring the club’s musical roots. Entourage is a circle of shared energy that brings together our artistic affinities, our diasporic influences, and our respective experiences behind the turntables. A space where we share, learn, and move forward together—both on and off the stage. Photographer: Boolbeny Akabb

Erika dreams on the cellular level, or perhaps of transdimensional intelligences moving through strange patterns on celestial objects, working towards a mysterious goal. Her connection to the dreamworld becomes concrete in her approach to music, simultaneously so solid and yet so ethereal.

Alongside Erika's artistic work, she is also a core force behind Interdimensional Transmissions, the Detroit-based label and party series, and the long-running No Way Back event, which takes place during Movement Festival weekend and has shaped generations of dancers and artists. We’re especially excited to welcome her to WHOLE’s ambient program, where she’ll present a 1.5-hour live set—an especially rare opportunity to experience her ambient work.

Photo by Andrew Charles Edman

wholefestival_description: EROS IN FURS (they/them) is a Berlin-based ritual artist, performer, and choreographer who makes shows that touch you in both body and spirit. Blending Butoh, drag, contemporary dance, and an obsession with death and rebirth, their work creates spaces for queer grief, desire, and the kind of liberation that feels like coming home. A devotee of Pluto, portals, and the liminal, EROS doesn’t just put on shows - they open thresholds. Photographer: @lonelyrodeostar

Berlin-based artists and producers AV FREYA and EVA VAN DIJK create experimental pop in their where textured instruments gently float through digital layers. Their collaboration began in the studio through client productions before unfolding on stages across the city with their original music. Their live sound blends floating guitars, shimmering synths and airy vocals, letting tension and tenderness collide in dreamlike softness. Catch them performing live at the ambient stage Saturday evening!

Fagatha Crispy is a creature shaken by the beautiful, sad and overwhelming world around her. Both parts a naive little clown and a fierce femme. Don't break her heart, she will break yours. Photographer: Elizaveta Bogachova

FARAONIKA is a singer, producer, and performer born in La Plata, Argentina. Her project blends pop fantasy, esoteric aesthetics, and sensual theatricality into a striking audiovisual identity. Rooted in queer expression, transformation, and desire, she has established herself as one of the most distinctive voices in the contemporary Argentine scene. Throughout her career, she has collaborated with artists and producers such as Fiah Miau, Cimarrón, Merca Bae, Chico Sonido, and Ojosfinos, expanding her sonic universe while maintaining an elegant yet defiant artistic identity.

Jay Jackson (fka.m4a) has built a solid reputation in the underground scene with a distinctive blend of euphoric and sensual House music. The London born and Berlin-based DJ has made an impact worldwide with filmed sets amassing over half a million streams online and he has played at venues and festivals like Timewarp, Sonar and Panorama Bar.

In 2024, he launched his own label and event series, CONTR4ST, which has become a home for emotionally driven dance music. So far, it’s released his debut EP Summer Nostalgia and the follow-up single through the heart, which came with a remix from Kyle Starkey as well as a variety of established `& up-and-coming artists.
But CONTR4ST is more than a label, it’s about creating a space where joy and love are put first, championing feelings of euphoria and that feeling that just makes you wanna hug your friends.
The first party at OHM in Berlin sold out, setting the tone for events that aim to take people on a journey: starting with warm, feel-good tracks that bring people together, moving into deeper, more introspective moments, then guiding the crowd back into connection again. CONTR4ST is about love, openness and joy; a place where everyone can feel good, seen and appreciated.

Born in London, music was always a big part of his life. He credits artists like the Spice Girls and Lil Kim — powerful women making bold statements — as some of his first musical loves, which eventually led him to start DJing in the queer scene back home.
“I was working on the Soho & East London queer circuit,” he says. “I was mainly doing guestlist and hosting but eventually they asked me to DJ and I spent a few years cutting my teeth on that scene.” After years of playing pop, a trip to Berlin in 2018 changed everything. Seeing Honey Dijon live flipped a switch. “I completely fell in love with the storytelling element of her set,” he says. “I spent the next year immersed in dance music culture and started playing the electronic room at queer events.”

He moved to Berlin in 2019, but just as things were starting to build, the pandemic hit. During lockdown, he started streaming sets from his apartment, which took off and helped him sign with an agency. Once clubs reopened, he landed a debut set at Panorama Bar.

On being mentored by Peggy Gou & regularly performing as her support act, he says “She’s helped me out so much and we have a really strong relationship. She calls me her daughter and I call her my mother”. From there, things moved fast, and he started playing bigger shows like Warehouse Project and Sonar and put more energy into making his own music.

“It’s important for me to make music that works in the club,” he says. “But most importantly, it’s about making something that comes straight from the heart. I think I’ve achieved that on my first EP and I can’t wait for people to connect with it.”

Now, with CONTR4ST growing and more events on the way, he’s looking ahead to a packed 2026 that includes sets at Tomorrowland, Mighty Hoopla, WHOLE, Milkshake, and Lost Village. Jay Jackson (fka.m4a) isn’t chasing trends — he’s building something personal, creative, and wide open to anyone ready to dance.

wholefestival_description: A true underground force, techno activist, and master of marathon sets, Freddy has been shaping the scene since the ’90s – from Rome’s iconic Virus movement to his legendary closing sets at Berghain. With an unmatched energy, deep musical knowledge, and a 360° approach to techno, he remains a driving force in the underground. As the head of KEY Vinyl, he continues to champion raw, unfiltered sounds with a pure, no-nonsense ethos. Expect intense, extended vinyl-only sets crafted strictly for the dancefloor. Photographer: Stini Roehrs

wholefestival_description: Once called ‘A defiant pillar of the Copenhagen scene’ in a low-rated YouTube comment, Barcelona-based Frederik Tollund is a DJ/producer firmly believing in the political and transformative power of dance floors. He is a co-founder of Group Therapy and a resident at the techno institution Den Anden Side in Copenhagen. As a DJ, Frederik is known for his high-energy closings and all-nighter sets with a UK-fused sound spanning leftfield techno, lofty tear-jerkers, and diva house – all centered around textures and emotions. His productions follow a similar hook-driven vein, with tracks released on Hypersoft, Radiant Love, and Another Name, supported by artists like Ben UFO, Jay Carder, and Juba. Photographer: Daniel Hjort

FRZNTE will perform on the performance stage during set changes of the next gig, keeping the energy high with dynamic music and playful stage presence. The set includes performative costume changes, audience interaction, and use of striking footwear and light elements such as LED/laser heels. Overall, the performance is an energetic, flirtatious, and immersive interlude that bridges the DJ sets. FRZNTE is a DJ, pole performer, and cultural producer working at the intersection of performance, music, and space. IIn her pole performances, she combines performative, choreographic, and sculptural elements, often referring to the architectural context. As a DJ, she blends Booty Beats, Disco, House, and eclectic favorites into high-energy, genre-bending sets – sometimes even bringing her pole on stage for a playful, hypnotic Pole Dance DJ Set.
soundcloud_description: 💄DJ & Pole Performer💄

wholefestival_description: A DJ, radio host and curator, her sets have enchanted vital club spaces such as Panorama Bar in Berlin and NYC’s Nowadays, as well as a span of festivals including Glastonbury, Horst, Melt and Dekmantel selectors. Kwarteng’s diverse taste and open-mindedness lead first, allowing her to fluidly slip through a mix of heady 80s and 90s NY house, disco interpretations that traverse diaspora and eras, percussive passages alongside boldly broken beats and, as the lights get lower, an undeniable acid undercurrent.  In an era of everything at once, Kwarteng weaves stories for the dancefloor that often look to the past, but always focus on possible futures.

wholefestival_description: GAZE is a Vienna-based queer platform and party series that creates immersive cultural experiences at the intersection of nightlife, art, and community. Rooted in the history of house music and queer expression, GAZE brings together music, fashion, performance, and visual art to build spaces where LGBTQIA+ identities are not only welcomed, but centered and celebrated.

GEGEN is a Berlin-based queer collective and event series exploring the intersections of techno, performance, fetish culture, and radical self-expression. Since its founding in 2011, GEGEN has created immersive club rituals where bodies, identities, and desires move beyond normative boundaries. Through large-scale events, artistic collaborations, and international tours, GEGEN brings together DJs, performers, visual artists, and audiences to create temporary spaces of transformation. Each edition revolves around a conceptual theme that investigates power, desire, politics, and the body.
soundcloud_description: Perceiving the idea of community as a demagogic form for bigger powers like religions, societies and markets to exercise their violence with the fake flag of politically correct partecipation, we do not wish to design a party just to give you a safe space and protection. We are not selling you the possibility to be “queer” for one night. We want to expose you to your own fears and pleasures by pushing you to understand how your own power mechanisms are produced. This is why we invite you to be GEGEN by performing your psychosis, by being the enemy of yourself, by thinking and being out of b/order and by destroying every rational thought.

wholefestival_description: Returning to host Whole's Got Talent for the 4th(5th?!) year running Gieza Poke is a community loving, hard-working, tender and vulgar bundle of DIY Drag joy! Host & producer of countless iconic drag shows in Berlin, she’s a singer, dancer, crafter, host and a full-time purveyor of filth. Auntie Gieza always puts the innuendo in crescendo! Photographer: RoniLugassi

Cute, sweaty, horny - all wrapped in radical care. Welcome to Group Therapy. A household name in the queer DIY rave scene of Copenhagen. Expect dedicated dancing, genre-fluid DJ’s, soft hearts and heaps of chaos energy. Be cunt and curious and leave a little transformed.

wholefestival_description: HAAi is known for her dynamic, tempo-shifting DJ sets. In 2024 HAAi played over 100 shows across five continents, 30 countries, and 91 cities. She took to the biggest stages at festivals, such as Glastonbury, Sonar, and EDC Las Vegas, while also delivering sets at local parties including Japan’s Rainbow Disco Club, Berlin’s RSO and Paris’ Iconic Rex Club, where she performed a year-long residency. In-between, HAAi was playing b2b’s with friends like KI/KI, Jennifer Cardini, The Blessed Madonna and Solomun. 2024 also saw the launch of UNiSON, her community focused party brand that featured surprise back-to-back sets with Boys Noize, DJ Boring and Romy, alongside a room programmed with emerging artists discovered via HAAi’s Submit to Play platform. UNiSON has become a platform for HAAi to uplift rising talent while creating an inclusive and boundary-pushing space for club culture. From her beginnings as a singer-songwriter in Western Australia to her rise as a globally renowned producer, songwriter and DJ, HAAi continues to push boundaries and redefine the possibilities of electronic music. Photographer: Imogene Barron

wholefestival_description:
HARD TON are the Italian duo with a larger than life acid sound and a surprising love of metal. Fused together in 2008 from the musical loins of Wawashi Deejay and heavy metal singer Max, Hard Ton have released through a plethora of class A labels such Permanent Vacation, Toy Tonics, Compost Disco, International Deejay Gigolo, Balkan Vinyl, Bordello A Parigi, Riotvan and many others. Their distinctive signature
sound is a unique blend of Chicago house, disco styled falsettos, crunchy acid basslines and a pinch of Italo disco (their origins do not lie!). In their show,
music and performance meet to create something that truly dares to be different: Max’s persona serves oversized queerness, fashion extravaganza and a
flawless vocal performance that makes every dancefloor starving for more. It’s time to party hard... ton. Photographer: Mauro “Wawashi” Copeta
soundcloud_description:
Info & Booking: elie@mensch-booking.com

HARD TON are the italian duo with the larger than life disco sound and a surprising love of metal. Fused together in 2008 from the musical loins of DJ Wawashi and heavy metal singer Max. Sylvester-styled falsettos conjure up memories of smoky dancefloors during the heady days of HI-NRG, which combined with a contemporary sound of accelerated beats and screaming acid basslines, shows a nod to the past can result in a slap across the face for the present. 
Using analogue and vintage gear, from the 303, 606, 707, Oberheim DMX, Korg Monopoly, MS20, Prophet V5, the music is as organic as synthetic music can be, their equipment living and dying by its own unpredictable rules.
(i-D Magazine)

wholefestival_description:
From intimate underground gatherings to packed main rooms, ISAbella has become a leading figure in today’s global electronic scene. As co-founder of the celebrated LGBTQ+ party, platform and collective MARICAS, she has crafted inclusive spaces that celebrate queer culture and foster connection both on and off the dancefloor. Her impact, however, extends far beyond the city – ISAbella is now a powerful presence on the international electronic music circuit, known for emotionally charged sets that blend house and techno into deeply
transformative dancefloor journeys. Originally from Cali, Colombia, ISAbella found her creative home in Barcelona, where she co-founded MARICAS in 2018—a party that quickly evolved into a multidisciplinary platform and record label. In 2020, she released her debut EP Audio Exótica on MARICAS Records and launched the livestream series The Art of Staying at Home, which offered a lifeline of connection and joy during lockdown. As a producer, ISAbella continues to explore hypnotic and percussive sonic landscapes that reflect her Latin American roots and club sensibilities. Her music has been featured on respected imprints like Peach Discs and MARICAS Records, with tracks that balance raw emotional intensity and bold rhythmic energy—further solidifying her voice as a singular artist in the global scene. Her reach has since gone global, with standout performances at world-
renowned festivals like Primavera Sound, Glastonbury, Sónar, Dekmantel, Dimensions and Lentekabinet. On the club circuit, she’s brought her distinctive sound to iconic, legendary venues including Berghain, Fabric, Nitsa and Fold. Beyond the decks, ISAbella continues to push for visibility and liberation within the scene—
championing a vision of club culture that is radical, joyful and deeply
human. Photographer: leoadef

wholefestival_description: Jacob Meehan is a Berlin- and LA–based DJ, producer, and part of the team organizing WHOLE  year-round. With a deep knowledge of the dance floor and the systems that hold it together, he’s quietly helped shape queer nightlife culture over the past two decades. Raised in 1990s rural America, a time and place not exactly overflowing with queer representation, creating and sustaining these spaces remains central to his work. Jacob received his education in Chicago, both formally and through his time at institutions like Smartbar and Gramaphone Records, where his connection to the city’s house music legacy took shape. His sound embodies the ethos of house, moving fluidly through techno, disco, and ambient, guided by momentum, restraint, and release. In 2026, Jacob stepped forward with a new body of self-released music on his imprint, Gay Groove Records.

wholefestival_description: JASSS is a Spanish sound artist, producer, and DJ with releases on labels such as AD-93, Ostgut Ton, and Never Sleep, aside from a residency in Herrensauna. Her retrofuturist style foregrounds bass and percussion, approaching but denying a techno orientation by embracing atmospheres of industrial pop, nu-metal distortion, and fuzzed-out R&B. wholefestival_description: Jeffrey Sfire is originally from suburban Detroit, where he was introduced to underground dance music and illegal warehouse parties as a teenager in the 90's. This escape from suburbia led him to the dancefloors of the city’s most talented DJs and musicians, who soon exposed him to a new culture of creative riches. Learning by example, Sfire’s taste and skills as a DJ developed naturally and he soon began playing out at parties, clubs and sleazy gay afterhours, entering covertly through back doors or with a fake ID. At the age of 18, a move to Chicago led Sfire to a whole other side of dance music history, including the Italo Disco and High Energy sounds of the 1980s. He spent afternoons digging through record store crates, meeting retired DJs and developing his musical palette. Jeffrey’s style is absolutely unique and clearly tied to the roots of his midwest upbringing and he can be found working the floor out with italo, high nrg, chicago house, and everything WBMX. Having held numerous club residencies, he regularly plays in LA, Detroit, Chicago, and New York, consistently offering a finely-tuned selection at clubs like Basement and Nowadays in NY, Panoramabar Berlin, and Hot Mass in Pittsburgh. He also has a hand in producing parties such as Detroit's infamous Movement weekend queer rager, Club Toilet, and collaborating with Justin Cudmore on a new monthly Sunday party at Signal (NY). Jeffrey is also a gifted producer and one half of production projects: Sfire (with Sophie), Looky Looky, and Pushing Movies with releases on labels such as Dark Entries, Cocktail D’Amore Music and Ultramajic, while his mixes have long been prized for their depth and quality and are often featured as RA mix of the day.

New York–born and Berlin–based, Jetconcept is a longtime contributor to kwia. Together with Lydo, he organized events at the space where he explores weaving indie R&B, hypnotic synths, submerged basslines, and drifting breakbeats. Jetconcept is part of KWIA’s 5-hour takeover, closing the Ambient Stage through to 5am Monday morning.

Their artistic essence combines elements of clubkid-drag, dance, theatre and punk. They aim at challenging elements of gender with their aesthetic while pushing the boundaries of traditional drag, the body, sexuality and identity. Photo by @paulinanoll

Her sets skillfully deploy the notion of sampling, and re-­‐blogging as DJ strategies, ecstatically mixing an array of influences that frolic at the boundary of genre-­‐ intuition and experimentation. At once an assertion of freedom and an ode to the evolutionary structure of electronic music subcultures, she aspires to the sublime in what can often only be described as witchcraft behind the decks; seance from the stage. wholefestival_description: Julie Desire embodies a new queer wave on the European electronic scene. She sets clubs and festivals across the continent ablaze with raw energy, percussive rhythms, and unapologetic sensuality. Blending progressive house, tribal house, and anything that could spark an orgy, her sound is an ode to desire, trans bodies, and sweat. Always high NRG, often sexy, her music is a visceral celebration of identity. A versatile producer, she’s released tracks on Maricas, BDE, Correspondant, and more. She’s also the co-founder of With Us, a queer party collective spotlighting trans and non-binary artists. Julie Desire is raw energy, a queer vision, and an unstoppable urge to make bodies move. wholefestival_description: KABULOM believes that music is a powerful tool that allows us to create spaces where a story can be told and valued. With his authentic musical selection, he manages to weave concepts, extensive research and passion into his sets, thus creating relevant performances. Creator and resident of the @mathosa.bh and @tumulto_co parties in Belo Horizonte, KABULOM seeks to place rhythm on the black perspective and his influences that derive from his own trajectory. His research is a dialogue between the way he sees the world and his connections with sound, which make him traverse different rhythms such as house, disco and even the rare kwaito in a unique way. Rhythm is what moves him, and through research he seeks to navigate among African children. Gaining knowledge of the history of black people, their struggles, their pain and their achievements. KABULOM has a special and passionate way of absorbing music. This ability is transmitted during his performances that reverberate his personality.

Jeffrey Sfire is a massive Kate Bush fan and tends to dive deep into her music, especially after heavy shit goes down. He’s always felt that fully immersing in her world can be kind of therapeutic, and for over a decade he’s wanted to play an all-Kate set. It finally came together at WHOLE 2024, a fever dream for the Kate freaks. 

So good, we’ve brought it back again. Come to the Ambient Stage Sunday afternoon to step into some shared Kate-ness, an invitation for those in need of something collectively lush collective and transcendent.

Alongside his ambient Kate Bush set, Jeffrey will also play the Beach stage on Saturday evening with Cocktail d’Amore; originally from Detroit and now based in New York, he is known for his impeccable record collection and for Sfire, his seminal project with Sophie.

Since then, they have released several productions, albums, and singles, including a remix of VTNC with Urias and Sensação de... with Irmãs de Pau. wholefestival_description: ketia they/them RAUM, Joyride, kaptcha and kwia\nketia is an up-to-no-good rave sweetheart with a keen ear for new and exciting music, alongside a curious, invested approach to community-building within the Lisbon, Berlin and worldwide club scene. Sonically they are enticed by syncopated drums and twisted rhythms, driven house with groove and breaks filled with emotion and nostalgia. marrying the RAVE energy of oldschool records with the freshness and excitement of the newest contemporary releases. ketia thrives on genre-bending electric and captivating mixing, connecting it all through vocal-infused journeys that allow for unique storytelling. Known for their infamous intimate invite-only kit ket events in Lisbon and one of the glitches behind the label and collective, kaptcha, that launched the xenolith series: a compilation type of release gathering funds for CASA T, a local shelter for trans folx. the debut of this series was recognized as DJ Mag’s top compilation of the year and Resident Advisor November’s Best Music. in Berlin, where they reside, they’re deep and sweetly entangled with the modern rituals happening at berlin's queer ambient listening bar kwia. ketia helps hold space for a different way of connecting through high fidelity sound and intimacy outside of the usual club dynamics. A resident at RAUM and Joyride, having made mixes for Honcho and DISCWOMAN, played Whole Festival, Atonal, Panorama Bar, Lente Kabinet and HORST, and a regular at queer parties and clubs around Europe and beyond like Basement, Lunchbox Candy, Group Therapy and UNFOLD on a never-ending tour, ketia feels very grounded and belonging in the worldwide club happenings.

wholefestival_description: Hailing from Caracas, Venezuela, Khloe is redefining her journey through transness and queerness by pushing sonic boundaries. Blending deep, hypnotic techno with raptor house and many other genres, she crafts a sound that honors Latin culture. Now rooted in Berlin’s electronic scene, Khloe uses music as a vessel for love, visibility, and education, unifying the spectrum to create an inclusive and transformative atmosphere. Khloe began her journey as an artist in Venezuela, shaping her sound and identity along the way. Now based in Berlin, she has made a name for herself as a resident DJ at GEGEN Berlin, Queer Pool (Hamburg), and MEID (Amsterdam, Berlin). Her latest project, Tanga, is a vibrant space where she and her community blend groovy techno with house music, creating an inclusive platform that uplifts minorities and celebrates diversity on the dance floor.
soundcloud_description:
Hailing from Caracas, Venezuela, Khloe is redefining her journey through transness and queerness by pushing sonic boundaries. 

Blending deep, hypnotic techno with raptor house and many other genres, she crafts a sound that honors Latin culture. 

Now rooted in Berlin’s electronic scene, Khloe uses music as a vessel for love, visibility, and education, unifying the spectrum to create an inclusive and transformative atmosphere.

Khloe began her journey as an artist in Venezuela, shaping her sound and identity along the way. 

Now based in Berlin, she has made a name for herself as a resident DJ at @gegenberlin and @Queer Pool (Hamburg)

Her latest project, @Tangaberlin, is a vibrant space where she and her community blend groovy techno with house music, creating an inclusive platform that uplifts minorities and celebrates diversity on the dance floor.

wholefestival_description: Hawaii-born, San Francisco-seasoned, and NYC-based musician Kim Ann Foxman has always been a capital-R Raver. For the three decades since she started to go out, the label owner, singer, and queer-musical-icon has evolved herself as a solo artist releasing records via her Firehouse & self:timer labels and as a vocalist with her club hits (“Creature”) and as a singer on the first two Hercules & Love Affair LPs. Her latest solo release “We Are Rhythm” featuring remix by Spray, has been an absolute club hit. Whether she is performing at nightclubs (Berghain to Bassiani), or festivals (Honcho to Pitch Arts,), Kim’s time-tested rave anthems will always be in her DJ-bag. Photographer: Augusto Silva Alliegro

wholefestival_description: You may now address Miss Kittin by her original moniker: Kittin. For decades, Kittin has pioneered electroclash, that grimy, irreverent collision of new wave and ‘90s techno. Singles “1982” and “Frank Sinatra” with The Hacker cemented her as an international DJ, both tracks heralded as anthems for the underground electroclash scene. Kittin decides on her collaborations without any preconceived ideas, she follows her instinct and works with new upcoming DJs as well as iconic figures she has been friends with for many years. She writes and sings without any safety net and delivers powerful works such as “Forever Raver” by ANNA and Kittin released in 2019 and “Naboo\ Photographer: Katja Ruge

Kontronatura’s sound is focused on percussive, hypnotic, and fast-paced beats such as Baile Funk, Kuduro, Bubbling, Dembow, Tribal Bass, Techno and Trance. His sonic research connects past and future by blending afro-diasporic rhythms with disruptive sounds from Latin America. It is driven by a deep commitment to the liberation of bodies and desires on the dancefloor, challenging linear narratives and invoking trance states through rhythm. As a resident of one of the biggest underground LGBTQ+ parties in Brazil (MAMBA NEGRA), Kontronatura has taken his sound across Brazil, Latin America, and Europe — performing at spaces like Goptun Festival, Novo Affair, Batekoo, ODD + Bassiani, Herrensauna, Tresor, RSO, Fusion Festival, Malajunta, International Chrome, Post Bar, Kosmos Festival, Universo Parallelo, Hiedrah, Desculonización, Metarave. In addition to that, his mixes aired on several notable platforms such as Boiler Room, Rinse France, NTS and others, being selected three times by Resident Advisor as mix of the day at 2021 , 2022 and 2025 he was selected again by Resident Advisor for a energetic B2B with Nick Léon at Carnaval Novo Affair in São Paulo, named \ In 2023, Kontronatura released his debut EP ORÍ via MAMBAREC — a sonic declaration of a personal quilombo. Across tracks like “IKU” and “BIYI,” he crafts an accelerated, jagged soundscape rooted in the pulse of the ghetto, conjuring future echoes, using sound as ritual, rupture, and refuge. ORÍ channels ancestry, exile, and rebirth through an uncompromising club lens. In 2025, he will release two more EPs in the second half of the year. In 2025, he began his residency at Oroko Radio in Accra, Ghana (quarterly) and Veneno Radio in São Paulo, Brazil (monthly). As a transmasculine artist, Achille (Kontronatura) understands that his body has political action in response to the lack of representation in the scene, in which trans bodies have little or no access. He takes back for himself the raw material of his existence as the greatest tool to position himself as an artist, being one representative of the transmasculine community in electronic music. Through his work, he is able to give back support to the trans community and open doors for it, actively participating in the Transmasculine Pride March in São Paulo and working on a project that provides free DJ training for trans men, transmasculine, and non-binary individuals.

wholefestival_description: Korgy is a Helsinki-based techno-trance phenomenon known for throwing the city’s most queued and gossip-stirring parties. With a strong focus on dancefloor energy and fun, it has grown into a community-driven movement that people travel afar to Helsinki to experience in the flesh. Korgy (est. 2021) has built its reputation on packed dancefloors, hedonistic aesthetics and a commitment to keeping the spirit playful. Throughout the years Korgy has hosted and shared lineups with artists like Patrick Mason, Narciss, Spikey Lee, Supergloss, Samantha Togni and Vinvar while collaborating with iconic queer collectives such as Gegen, PRNCPTL, Bedside, Boudica & Kinky Sundays at clubs like KitKatClub & RSO in Berlin, FOLD in London and Den Anden Side in Copenhagen. At the core of Korgy is supporting both local and international artists - established or newcomer, the vibe is what counts. Alongside it’s Helsinki-based residents, the collective also includes international forces such as Schacke and Cristian Marras. The essence of Korgy lies in creating a welcoming bubble of uninhibited self-expression. It’s a dreamlike space where people can embrace their glow without judgment, shed their inhibitions, and lose themselves in the collective energy of a likeminded crowd. Korgy has quickly captured the attention of the city’s nightlife and continues their pioneer work to shape the queer nightlife in Helsinki. In just a few years, it has established itself among the new wave of European club culture delivering euphoric, banging dance music and celebrating freedom, queerness, and a healthy dose of not taking themselves too seriously. Korgy is best understood the way it was meant to be experienced: in the flesh!

wholefestival_description: Founded and self-managed in February 2022 by four artists - Mascare, Soa de Muse, Bili Bellegarde, and Grand Soir - La Bouche is a queer cabaret of multiple and complementary identities, born in a concrete basement in Paris.\nLa Bouche Cabaret positions itself as a joyful and living archive of collective histories, driven by the need, duty, and desire to transmit the memories of intersectional struggles and the rights of minorities.\nOn its stage, the artists sing and speak of yesterday and tomorrow, of lesbians, racism, queerness, social class, and systemic violence; but above all, of hope and love. From Monique Wittig to Céline Dion or Sexy Sushi, through Michel Berger and Malavoi, the performances are constantly renewed according to current events, tributes, and the individual inspirations of each artist; yet all remain connected by a shared essence: a sexy, punk, pop, sincere, poetic, and always political imagination.

An ongoing homage to the trio's shared obsession for the warm nostalgia and groove of queer nightlife. This single thread carries through the event series' intentionality with sound, aesthetics and community values - always in the name of joy and reinstating a sense of intimacy within Berlin's vast club scene. Byron first teamed up with S- candalo as residents for his Radiant Love party series, with the duo's classicly camp and euphoric sound levelling his signature prog, trance and hard-house style. La Noche marks a true collaboration of the three's distinct vibes, creating a space that actualises the elative state their sets collectively induce.

Lakuti’s passion for music extends far beyond the dancefloor, both musically and politically. A DJ and musician first and foremost, the Panorama Bar resident is also a curator, producer, Uzuri artist agency and label head, and a respected educator and voice within the scene. Born and raised in Soweto, South Africa, Lakuti’s early influences came from her family’s rich record collection, spanning soul, funk, disco, jazz and South African sounds. Her taste soon expanded through Johannesburg’s club culture, where sounds from New York, Chicago and Detroit left a lasting impact. Now based in Berlin, Lakuti is an internationally respected DJ, playing clubs, festivals and arts spaces worldwide, while holding a long-standing presence at Berghain/Panorama Bar. Alongside Tama Sumo, she co-founded Bring Down the Walls and Your Love, celebrating Black and LGBTIQ+ communities on and beyond the dancefloor. Photographer: Alison Miller
soundcloud_description: Dj , Promoter , Founder of Uzuri Recordings + Uzuri artist Bookings & Management as well as Co founder alongside Tama Sumo of Bring Down The walls .

wholefestival_description: LATINEO is a collective created by queer South American people in Barcelona, designed as a gathering space for women, diverse identities, and dissident communities, centered around electronic music and rhythms from the continent.

Laure Croft, a Dutch-born DJ now based in Berlin, is a key figure in the city's techno scene, known for her 'keep it sxxy' mantra. Her electrifying sets blend early 2000s tracks with contemporary releases, creating a dynamic and sensual sound that’s both recognizable and unique. Though not strictly a vinyl purist, Laure finds deep excitement in using Technics turntables, enhancing the authenticity of her performances. Having launched her own label, @Sxxyrecs, she continues to shape the electronic music world, committed to inclusivity and staying true to the essence of techno.

wholefestival_description: Producer, DJ and architect, she began her artistic development in 2016. She has been part of Panal Records since 2017, and has released her music on the label, as well as on Tensa Records, Modismo Label and TSA. Her DJ sets are characterised by intense energy, with driving percussion and vibrant synthesis, exploring different shades and genres ranging from techno and trance to Latin American rhythms. She has brought her music to local raves, parties across different regions of the country, and major festivals such as CREAMFIELDS, DGTL, and AURA alongside Amelie Lens and DAME. In addition, she actively participates in the production of events within the local scene, collaborating with projects such as Panal Records, Hecatombe, among others. She is also part of the collective WIP (Women In Power).

wholefestival_description: Lie Ning is a London based pop singer who makes music for grown ups and music for Black and Queer people from the perspective of a Black and Queer person. They will be performing a dynamic pop set, taking the audience on a journey that spans over seven years of released music. The main feeling to take away from it, is a sense of hope without denying the dire situation our world finds itself in. There will be space to dance, cry, laugh, feel yourself, feel us.

Linapary is a powerhouse DJ and artist who blends perreo, guaracha, and tribal rhythms with an electrifying mix of Latin beats and cutting-edge electronic sounds. From Colombia’s Caribbean coast to Madrid, she curates high-energy sets that fuse the best of Latin music with global electronic vibes. Expect a fierce celebration of femme power, inclusion, and non-stop dancing as Linapary delivers the ultimate sound experience, shaking up the stage and culos!

wholefestival_description: Leaning into several layers of dance music—from metallic techno, angelic trance, and squishy acid to cosmic bleep—Lolsnake (she/they) fuses a maelstrom of textures into her sets, stirring up heady atmospheres and hypnotic moods on the dance floor. With a fluid, genre-defiant approach, she’s carved out a distinct sonic signature: playful, cerebral, emotionally deep, and magnetically unpredictable. A regular at Berghain, Lolsnake is also the founder of Weeeirdos, a queer platform that champions subversive joy, curiosity, and community. As a DJ and visual artist, her multifaceted background fuels a perspective that’s both boundary-pushing and deeply rooted in connection. Photographer: Brad Feuerhelm
soundcloud_description:
LOLSNAKE (She/they) @weeeirdos

Leaning into the hypnotic, the tension-building, and the euphoric release—Lolsnake (she/they) does not simply play to a room, she creates it. With a fluid, genre-defiant approach, her sets move between the reduced and the raw, the dramatic and the unpredictable, stirring up heady atmospheres and hypnotic moods on the dancefloor. Driving rhythms and intricate sound design sit alongside moments of startling emotional depth — a sonic signature that is at once intense and deeply feeling, cerebral and magnetically unpredictable.

A regular at Berghain, Lolsnake is also the founder of Weeeirdos, a queer platform that champions subversive joy, curiosity, and community. As a DJ and visual artist, her multifaceted background fuels a perspective that is both boundary-pushing and deeply rooted in connection — her sets a constant balance between classic dancefloor accessibility and a desire to push at the edges of genre convention.
In 2025, Lolsnake took her sound global, performing at Bassiani (Tbilisi), Basement NYC, WHOLE Festival, and returned to Berghain for several celebrated sets that reflected her evolving sonic palette. Her genre-crossing energy has also been showcased in guest mixes for Mixmag, DJ Mag, The Lot Radio, and Crack Magazine.

Weeeirdos, which began in 2017 as a hybrid event combining live music, performance and visual art, has since become one of Berlin's most vital queer dance platforms. In 2023, it moved from OHM to a residency at Säule (Berghain). That same year, Boiler Room invited Lolsnake to curate a Weeeirdos live stream from Berlin's dungeon-like Nåpoleon Komplex. Lolsnake has since been asked to curate key events on the Berghain Mainfloor, The Ground (Space) Miami, Radion Amsterdam, and more.

Born in California to Iraqi parents and raised in Oakland, Lolsnake's musical roots trace back to classical training in clarinet, saxophone, and bass clarinet. Her teenage years were shaped by a love of Depeche Mode, Underworld, The Knife, and the Arabic tapes of Oum Kalthoum and Fairouz. A move to Manchester in 2010 to study neuroscience immersed her in the UK's DIY rave scene. Berlin followed in 2014, where she found a deep sense of belonging in the city's queer nightlife.

After a decade in Berlin, Lolsnake's sets have matured into shimmering constellations of rhythm — reduced and raw one moment, warm and euphoric the next, with mischievous curveballs at just the right moment. With plans to launch the Weeeirdos label and take the project on a global tour, she remains a powerful force in contemporary club culture: driven by feeling, rooted in community, and guided by no rules but her own.
