wholefestival_description: In her short but explosive DJ career, LOVEFOXY, the born-and-bred Berliner, has solidified her status as one of the most exciting new faces in house music. Immersed in Berlin’s legendary electronic music scene since her teenage years (her mother opened the city’s first house club, frequented by icons like David Bowie), LOVEFOXY channels a deep-rooted appreciation for the euphoric sounds of’90s house into every performance. Her meteoric rise is nothing short of extraordinary. By 2023, just two years behind the decks, she debuted at Boiler Room and Berghain, sharing lineups with luminaries such as Eliza Rose, Jacques Greene, Ben UFO, Joy Orbison, Seth Troxler, DJ Heartstring, and Floorplan. She has since become an internationally touring DJ, lighting up venues like KOKO, Robert Johnson, and Brunch Elektronik, as well as festivals such as DGTL, ADE/Intercell, Tomorrowland, and Awakenings. 2024 was a landmark year for LOVEFOXY, with the release of her debut EP, Burning Down the Sluthouse, on Nervous Records and a follow-up remix pack earlier this year featuring Ian Pooley, Malugiand Evissimax. Her forth coming schedule includes two EP’s on Manchester’s Warehouse Project Records and Radio Slave’s imprint Rek’d. LOVEFOXY has been taking her artistry to new heights with a residency at Paris’s iconic club Badaboum (featuring guests DJ Spit and Gabrielle Kwarteng). With an ever-growing devoted following that intersects with fans of LSDXOXO and Honey Dijon, she is not just a DJ but a cultural force—an embodiment of house music’s vibrant, boundary-pushing future Photographer: Tereza Mundilová

Luca Bonamore (he/him) (b. 1996, Rome) is a choreographer, dancer, and performer based in Vienna. He graduated in 2023 with a BA in Contemporary Dance Pedagogy from the Music and Arts Private University of the City of Vienna. Moving between theatre stages and queer club spaces, he develops his own choreographic works while remaining deeply rooted in the club environments where his early career first took shape. His works include LAMENTATIONS, Silent Lovers, and Heavy Duty. Most recently, he appeared in the theatre production The Boys Are Kissing and presented Boys(friend) together with actor Paul Winkler. He is currently developing his new piece Schwulenduett. Alongside his artistic work, Luca works as a personal assistant for people with cognitive and multiple disabilities. “She is an icon, she is a legend, and she is THE MOMENT”

Lucky Lube weaves together the playful, the sexy, and the unhinged. Groove is in the heart, percussion in the soul and queerness is the perspective. Co-founder of Group Therapy, a non-profit DIY queer party rooted in care, sensuality, and collective release, and resident at Den Anden Side, Copenhagen’s most vital underground club, he plays with a sensitivity that melts the emotional armour, guiding dancefloors into states of shared feeling. Lucky Lube doesn’t just play music. He lubricates the room. Navigating the full arc of house music in all its deviant subgenres, from sultry minimalism to sweat-soaked euphoria, he guides rhythm and mood with a light, intuitive touch. Deep, sexy, and percussive, with rolling basslines, crisp beats, and queer-coded edits that are blended with emotional precision, always tuned to the energy of the room. Every set is an exercise in tension and release: music chosen not to dominate, but to connect and transform. Lucky Lube brings an intention to the booth that prioritises the room: reading it, teasing it, and slowly melting its edges. With many years of experience curating queer nightlife, his approach goes beyond technical skill. His sets reflect a sensitivity for pleasure, for expression, for unity. He carries fragments of Group Therapy beyond its home base: traces of care, ecstasy, and queer joy, reassembled in real time. Appearances include sets at Maricas Boiler Room (Vienna), Radiant Records (Copenhagen), Cuddles and Lecken (Berlin), Outra Cena (Lisbon), TES (Tbilisi), and tours in Australia and Mexico. He plays b2b with party starting auteurs like Jennifer Loveless and Angel D’Lite and his recent mixes for SMUT Press, Rinse FM and Maricas highlight his distinct sound. Photographer: André Hansen

wholefestival_description: M8NSE is a Chilean artist, journalist, and DJ based in Barcelona. Her sound bridges Latin bass, techno, ambient, and breaks, weaving fast-paced percussion with emotionally driven atmospheres. Deeply connected to queer and migrant communities, she is the founder of LATINEO, a platform amplifying South American electronic music within the FLINTA diaspora. Recent and upcoming highlights include MIRA Festival 2025 and Primavera Sound 2026. Photographer: Redporteros

Majdolen is a Syrian-born DJ, producer, and curator based in Berlin. A resident at Power Dance Club and co-founder of Substation, she has become a fixture on lineups across the city while appearing in European cities such as Copenhagen, Amsterdam, and Athens. Known for emotionally driven sets moving between techno B-sides, trance-drenched psychedelia, rough-cut house, and shimmering disco hybrids, her debut EP Spirals (Umay Works) and other releases extend the same vision into her productions.\n\nPhoto by @feydangels

wholefestival_description: This Brooklyn-based DJ/Producer/Creative Director, hailing from Zahle Lebanon, owns the underground scene with his signature sound merging Arabic influences over cross-genre sets. He is a resident and co-founder of FUNCTION NYC, a space that has cultivated a dance floor honoring the natural, vibrant diversity of New York City. Photographer: Zach Doung

Maricas was born in January 2018 due to several discussions about the lack of a night-out in Barcelona that combined electronic music and the queer scene. If we look back on history, the techno rave community started with the help of the LGBTQ+ misfits of the 80s. Both communities have very similar values, freedom, youth and underground culture. Our home is Barcelona, but we aim to take Maricas all over the globe. MARICAS was founded in 2028, in Barcelona, by Eloisa Blitzer, Gina Guasch & ISAbella due to the lack of a night that played electronic music and was a safer space for FLINTA individuals. The first party was thrown in January 2018 and to launch it, after organising several nights, a huge community of friends, artists, lovers ended up growing into a family of mutual care and support. Over the years, MARICAS has grown into one of Europe's most recognised queer cultural projects, with over 150 events programmed and produced across Europe and America. Achievement-wise, they have done three Boiler Rooms, held a residency on Crack Magazine & EOS Radio, and a residency on RINSE FM (London). They were recognised as Party of the Year 2024 by Resident Advisor. the most prestigious accolade in electronic music media, and were invited to speak on an RA Exchange dedicated entirely to the project. They have been part of Whole Festival, Primavera Sound, Dimensions Festival, Body Movements, Dour Festival and Manifesta 15, the European Nomadic Biennial. In Barcelona, they have achieved three sold-out nights at Nitsa with 2,500 people per event. Internationally, MARICAS has thrown parties in Stockholm, Helsinki, Copenhagen, Ibiza, Valencia, Paris, Amsterdam, Rotterdam, Vienna, New York, Brussels Croatia, London, Berlin, Munich, Antwerp, Oslo & Madrid. Collaborated with institutions such as Munch Museum Oslo, CA2M Madrid and BAM Festival Barcelona. Their work has been featured in Dazed, Gay Times, Resident Advisor, Crack Magazine, Vice, Metal Magazine, Número Netherlands and more. They have also successfully launched their record label, MARICAS Records, both digitally and on vinyl. Photographer: Xula

wholefestival_description: Mars O10C is a non binary DJ, promotor and activist based in Paris, Hors Sol resident and member of With Us collective, which supports trans and non-binary artists. They organize events that bridge activism and club culture, creating community-driven parties with a focus on ambitious curation. Mars O10C is multifaceted, both in their personality and their musical selection. Their artistic project is built around two worlds : one part of them cherishes ambient and downtempo rhythms, while another, irresistibly drawn to dancefloors, collects 90s and 2000's house, percussive and progressive tracks. The common thread is the emotion that emerges from their sets, a mix of sensuality and nostalgia, the love of\natmospheric, trippy and groovy sounds. With a USB key in hand and a vinyl bag slung over the shoulder, Mars O10C embodies an intense curiosity and a love of craft and digging, while approaching music with an open heart. Resident on Radio Sofa since 2022, showcasing the diversity of their musical interests, Mars O10C also loves collaborating with other artists. Photographer: Solisse

wholefestival_description: Maryama Luccioni, known by her stage name Maryisonacid, is a French-Congolese DJ, promoter, curator, and founder of the African Acid Is The Future label and party based in Berlin. With a distinctive approach to DJing, she seamlessly blends diasporic African sounds with groovy electronic tracks and techno, a style deeply influenced by her upbringing in Paris’s Barbès district and her involvement in Berlin’s diverse party scene. Maryisonacid’s passion for music transforms each set into a unique and cinematic journey, defying traditional conventions by incorporating elements of her heritage with an experimental, punk ethos. Some of her notable performances include Berghain’s Panorama Bar, Contact Tokyo, and Printworks London, alongside her residency at Tresor Berlin, where she delivers skillful storytelling with the energy and ease of an experienced selector.\n\nWe are excited to welcome her back to the ambient stage with her newer alias \

wholefestival_description: There’s an ongoing lust for dialogue when it comes to Massimiliano Pagliara – be it through his collaborations and solo productions, during his DJ sets, with his own music label Funnuvojere Records or on a personal level. The world of dance, movement and communication is deeply rooted in Massi’s life – when you know how to move yourself, you easily know how to move others. Photographer: Paul Mpagi Sepuya
soundcloud_description:
Massimiliano Pagliara was born in Tricase (Lecce) in the deep south of Italy. Already as a child he listened to diverse types of music: rap, classical, reggae, dub and 60s rock'n'roll. Coming from this small village, he and his friends created a small musical world for themselves, spending a lot of time every day playing records in his basement and at private parties.
Upon moving to Milan in 1997, Massi embarked on an academic career and eventually earned his diploma in theatre, dance and choreography at the Civica Scuola di Teatro Paolo Grassi in 2001. While discovering the fashionable and alternative club scene of the city, his imagination was captured by experimental electronic music such as Aphex Twin, Boards Of Canada, Autechre and Mira Calix. Naturally these avant-garde sounds eventually found their way into Massi's original body of work.
After finishing school he ventured to Europes underground capital Berlin, where he continued his studies in contemporary dance, but also dived into the city's explosive techno nightlife, particularly the renowned OstGut club. Massi's interest in electronic music found its peak in 2003 when he finally began to pursue DJing with a passion. Ever since he's been spinning records across the world as well as some of the most popular underground venues in Germany – among them Tape Club, SchwuZ, Tresor, ://about blank, Robert Johnson, and of course Berghain / Panorama Bar, where he holds a residency.
Massi describes his style as an eclectic mix of house, disco, electro and techno – one may call it current-yet-classic. DJing has since led to productio. These days Massi lets his obsession with analog synths run wild – usually he's holed up in his apartment studio, working on his latest productions. 2011 saw the release of his debut album Focus For Infinity (on Live At Robert Johnson), followed by his sophomore LP With One Another in 2014. February 2017 saw his debut release on Ostgut Ton with the EP Time And Again. Since then, Massi has worked on various EPs, remixes, single track, all culminating in the late 2018 release of his third full-length solo album, titled Feel Live. February 2020 saw his debut on Munich's label Permanent Vacation with his new EP Nothing Stays In One Place For Long, in 2021 he's added the third part of his Uncanny Valley label trilogy with the EP Connection Lost Pt. 3. His latest album See You In Paradise releases in October 2022, once again with Permanent Vacation.
But he’s not always alone: Massi has been collaborating with luminaries around town such as gui.tar, Snax, Daniel Wang, Lemercier & The Lala By, Discodromo as well international artists. This lead to several side projects over the last decade, such as [sic!] with the two members of gui.tar, The Rimshooters with the Brazilian DJ and producer Rotciv, Egyptian Nipples with the multi-instrumentalist Jules Etienne, Banana Moon with Luigi Di Venere, Couple Looking with Chris Cruse to name just a few.
There's an ongoing lust for dialogue when it comes to Massimiliano Pagliara – be it through his collaborations and solo productions, during his DJ sets, with his own music label Funnuvojere Records or on a personal level. The world of dance, movement and communication is deeply rooted in Massi's life – when you know how to move yourself, you easily know how to move others.

wholefestival_description: Photographer: The Sinful Son

The best thing about MEEK? There is a point to her. She is as if the comedy queen, Daisy May Cooper stumbled into a charity shop, found a bunch of glittering second hand couture, dolled herself up shamelessly in it, injected the raw spirit of Freddie Mercury and emerged, Mr Ben style, as a fully-fledged MEGASTAR in the making out of the changing rooms, then lead a troubadour of misfits singing down the high street.\nThere is not what you might call a shortage of self-confidence in MEEK. As she sings herself, on a calling card anthem which is sure to become the earworm of the nation once unleashed upon its airwaves: “I’m so fucking fabulous.”

Memphy is a DJ shaping a distinctive lane within the global club scene, known for her sharp, high-energy sets that fuse percussive club, bass-heavy sounds, and diasporic rhythms. With a refined yet instinctive approach to selection, she builds tension through fast-paced transitions and unexpected blends, keeping dancefloors locked from start to finish.\nHer sound moves between intensity and groove—balancing hard-hitting moments with fluid, rhythmic shifts that feel both raw and intentional. Rooted in underground culture, Memphy brings a fearless and forward-thinking energy to every set, creating spaces that are immersive, physical, and emotionally charged.\nWhether playing intimate clubs or larger stages, Memphy delivers with precision and presence, establishing herself as a compelling force within the new wave of international DJs.

Loretta Chantiri, also known as MIKITA, is a DJ, producer, drummer, and percussionist from Mexico City. She began her musical journey more than 10 years ago, although she officially entered the electronic music scene in 2023. Since then, she has performed at clubs, venues, parties, and festivals in Mexico including Bar Oriente, Brutal MX, [SIC], Rico Club, Pride Together, Foro Indie Rocks, Pepsi Center, Auditorio BB, EDC Mexico and, of course, Pervert MX, where she is resident. Her sets move seamlessly through the sounds of classic disco, nu disco, house, tech house, Latin, tribal, hardgroove, and techno, blending styles with confidence and energy. With each performance, MIKITA continues to refine her sound: dynamic, versatile, and made for moving. Also, as part of the collective and label In Gad We Trust, she recorded and released several tracks that document the evolution of her sound from her beginnings as a producer to the present day. Titles such as HRT, AINIY, Lick It, Muévete, Analog, and more are available on all digital platforms. With years of experience behind the decks, MIKITA confidently delivers a unique, adaptable, recognizable, and iconic sound and style. Photographer: Alejandra Miriel

Her sets are bold, sensual, and rhythm-driven, blending hard-hitting club sounds with a distinct sense of identity and storytelling. Drawing from international scenes and underground movements, Mina creates sonic journeys that are both physical and expressive. Through Club Stamina, she has built a reputation for curating spaces that celebrate freedom, community, and unapologetic self-expression—bringing that same energy to every stage she steps on. Photographer: Saint

She is known for her recognisable vocals and imaginative lyrics and for exploring the blends between Neo Perreo, Reggaeton, Dancehall and more in her releases. Photographer: Pedro Quintana

wholefestival_description: N0L4.44 is a DJ from the Caribbean islands, raised from childhood on the sounds of Guadeloupe and Martinique. N0L4.44 has developed a unique sonic identity that she defines as “global black club music”: a playground where dancehall, baile funk, electronic music, and Afro-Caribbean and Latin influences converge. Each set is conceived as an intense, organic, and boundless journey, where sounds intersect, collide, and ignite. With a resolutely underground, queer, festive, and deeply energetic approach, N0L4.44 transforms the club into a space for total letting go. Here, there are no half-measures: bodies sweat, hips sway freely, and identities assert themselves. Dance becomes political, joyful, and liberating. With three years of experience under her belt, N0L4.44 is quickly making a name for herself on the Marseille scene, playing at iconic venues such as Palais Longchamps (Marsatac), Espace Julien, Cabaret Aléatoire, Unité.22, and many others. Her energy and her set selection have also taken her to several French cities, steadily establishing her place within the alternative and inclusive club scenes.

International chantreusse extraordinaire. Camp diva; a chameleon of mischief and glamour. Drag dissident, gender clown, conjurer of sacred profanity. All in the name of the transformative power of collective imagination. Photographer: Javier Alejandro Cerrada

Natuta, the Tbilisi artist has been carving out her own lane with a fierce blend of riotous machine funk, punk-laced rhythms and high-voltage club energy. As the founder of Eau de Cologne, the queer party series held at Bassiani, she has become a vital force in her city’s underground, shaping spaces built on freedom, sweat and collective release. Natuta’s sets are expansive and instinctive, driven more by energy and atmosphere than strict genre lines. Raw, playful, and at times tipping into trippy punk savagery. With international appearances and a growing presence beyond Tbilisi, Natuta continues to carry the spirit of her local scene wherever she plays. As she puts it simply: “I like music and music likes me.” Photographer: by Ganem Haiek
soundcloud_description: I like music and music likes me.

wholefestival_description: When Nazira Kassenova started DJing and throwing parties in her native Kazakhstan, the lack of a club culture or community was as much an opportunity to embrace as an obstacle to overcome. Without the burden of assumptions and hardened expectations, she seized the chance to push sounds and create spaces that reflected an idealized vision of electronic music and its social possibilities. In doing so, Nazira became a beacon for the growth of a local scene in her hometown Almaty, and achieved international renown as an in-demand DJ for her omnivorous yet resolute musical approach. She ignites dancefloors with acidic, dynamic, and psychedelic sets, rooted in techno but stretching effortlessly beyond the genre's confines. Nazira began DJing in Almaty in 2014, and threw the first parties of the ZVUK collective she founded in 2016. After a transformative, fiery performance at Unsound in Krakow that same year, she curated and performed at their satellite event Dislocation Almaty in 2017, and has since served in a similar curatorial role for the first-ever Boiler Room in Kazakhstan in 2021, alongsider other members of ZVUK. In-between, she honed her skills as a resident DJ at Berlin's Room 4 Resistance club night and via shows on Radio Coméme and Rinse FM, while becoming a reliable presence on scene-defining mix series from Dekmantel and Resident Advisor. Nazira's efforts building an open-minded scene in a conservative country like Kazakhstan have been covered by outlets ranging from Resident Advisor and Pitchfork to WePresent and the New York Times. But even with all this outside acclaim and attention, she continues to live in Almaty and operate ZVUK, committed to the city's development through that collective as well as her 4x4 DJ School, which informs and inspires locals to unlock their own creativity through DJing. Photographer: Tima Baldin

wholefestival_description: Nsasi is a fearless sound sculptor, core member of the Nyege Nyege collective, and a founding member of the radical ANTI-MASS collective alongside Authentically Plastic and Turkana. Known for their high-intensity DJ sets that blend hard drum, Singeli, gqom, electro, and experimental club textures, Nsasi creates relentless, addictive dancefloor pressure that often pushes 160 BPM and beyond. With fearless mixing and an unpredictable approach to rhythm, they turn every set into a live journey, navigating through radically different tempos and genres, from skittered acid to pulsating percussion. An Enfant Terrible of Uganda’s deeply conservative heartland, Nsasi’s path to rebellion was clear from the start. Growing up in the picturesque town of Jinja, they earned an early reputation for blunt refusal of authority—once excommunicated from a seminary in their youth, Nsasi looked to their icon, the scandalous 1980s pop sensation Tshala Mwana, for guidance on how to live and perform dangerously. It’s this same audacity and conviction that fuels their sound. Nsasi’s debut album Coinage (Hakuna Kulala, 2024) is a dynamic, industrial rasp that expertly meshes rhythms from across the musical spectrum into a coarse, dancefloor-ready frame. A rallying cry for outsiders and the queer community, the album mischievously perverts East African traditional sounds, considering their revolutionary potential within a new context. “Coinage” is a fluid, breathtaking record that peers into an uncertain future, balancing the chaotic energy of noise and industrial music with the precision of club forms. The percussive mastery on Coinage is on full display—whether it’s the polyrhythmic anarchy of “Gabvla,” with hand drums and psychedelic electroid wheezes, or the mind-bending tribal-industrial clash of “Endoongo”—Nsasi pushes the limits of what dance music can express. The record is coherent in its complexity, brilliantly pulling together seemingly disparate influences into a unified, arresting sound. With Coinage, Nsasi operates outside borders and restrictions, creating a sound that reflects both the turbulent present and a fearless future. But it’s on the decks where Nsasi’s true force emerges, crafting sets that aren’t just about music—they’re a declaration of freedom, resistance, and power. From Kampala’s underground to global dancefloors, Nsasi doesn’t just DJ—they detonate Photographer: Louis Headlam

wholefestival_description: With a distinctive energy that stands out in Santiago’s current scene and drawing on sonic influences from ’90s techno, NSPERGER seeks to connect with their audience through fresh and genuine mixes. They have had the honour of sharing the stage with artists such as Kittin, Bashkka, Pablo Bozzi, Phase Fatale, VTSS, Ellen Allien, Hector Oaks, Freddy K, Patrick Mason, Vladimir Dubyshkin, Ki/Ki, SPFDJ, Anetha, DJ Gigola, Sara Landry, U.R.traxx and many more. Thanks to an invitation to Whole Festival in Berlin, the most important queer festival in Europe, they have also had the opportunity to perform in Berlin, Barcelona, Madrid, Rotterdam, and on well-known radio platforms such as Loone, PNL and Operator. In addition, they have performed at the globally renowned BASSIANI in Tbilisi, Georgia. They have also brought their music to stages such as FA GOT in Argentina, PDC, Pornceptual & Gegen in Berlin, Basement in New York, RAVE3000 in Argentina, Brutal in Mexico, Baum in Colombia, DGTL, Creamfields, Boiler Room, 240KM and Herrensauna in Santiago, among others. Nsperger has developed their career as a musical selector since 2004, performing in key spaces for dissident communities and queer audiences. They began with their first residency at Clubizarre and are currently a resident at Fiesta Dame. In their sets, they create a sonic landscape that transports listeners from an abandoned roadside factory to a dancefloor filled with sweaty bodies. Their main musical influences come from punk, acid and electroclash, and they have used these influences to build their own distinctive style. Their musical selection is eclectic, exploring everything from the subtleties of electronic music to rhythms that provoke euphoric screams, aiming to deliver an auditory experience that is both exciting and fun.

wholefestival_description: “Nyēbra” is an insult his father used to call him when he was a child; it means “caiman.” This term embodies the chaotic, ancestral hyper-energy that Nyēbra seeks to convey in their sets. A Franco-Burkinabe DJ based in Marseille, they explore their vision of the ultimate Afro Rave, blending their diverse influences—ranging from their queer identity in the Ballroom scene to their West African roots. Nyēbra jumps from one genre to another: from Super Lite Feet to Tekuduro, Cardio Jersey Club, Ghetto House, Vogue Beats, and above all, Afro Techno. It’s an intense mix for energetic dancers. Photographer: Boolbeny Akabb

Dance music has often romanticized the idea of the anonymous artist who quietly lurks in the shadows, but Octo Octa (a.k.a. Maya Bouldry-Morrison) has no interest in hiding who she is. She's an openly autobiographical artist, and one who's eager to tell her story as a radical queer trans woman polyamorous witch. The New Hampshire-based DJ, producer and live performer has been active for nearly a decade, but in recent years she's leveled up, fueled by the rising tide of North America's queer DJ scene, not to mention the success of her albums Where Are We Going? (2017) and Resonant Body (2019). Though her music has appeared on labels such as Naive, Honey Soundsystem, Technicolour and 100% Silk, Resonant Body was released on T4T LUV NRG, a label she runs alongside her romantic and DJ partner Eris Drew. The two can often be found going back-to-back in the booth, and together they've created a platform that actively celebrates trans love and visibility, along with the potent healing magic of the dancefloor. House, techno and breakbeat are Octo Octa's genres of choice, although these kinds of descriptors ultimately feel a bit clinical given the intensely personal nature of her music. Gravitating toward the ecstatic, the joyful and the openly emotional, it's no wonder that Bouldy-Morrison's uniquely uplifting vision has been enthusiastically welcomed on dancefloors around the globe, including those at world-renowned festivals like Sónar, Dekmantel and Sustain Release. While her path—especially outside the club—has often been (and continues to be) challenging, it's in dance music that Octo Octa has found connection, self-realization and true love. She's got a story to tell, and it's powerful. Photographer: Marysia Swietlicka

The London DJ is known for her high energy club sets which masterfully travel through genres - freeform blends of bassy floor busting tracks live alongside blissful synth-heavy house, with techno, jungle and electro also weaved into the mix. Everything Williams plays is intentional and shining with personality. Her multidimensional radio shows on NTS have garnered a loyal and global following, while her idiosyncratic sound has taken her across the world to festivals like Unsound, Horst and Draaimolen, and made her a resident at the New York club Nowadays starting in 2024. wholefestival_description: A passionate digger with a background in musicjournalism and event curation, Olivia approaches her sets as narrative journeys – sonorous, layered, and emotionally resonant. From Brooklyn to Kiev, she’s queered consciousness through sound,\ncreating dance floors that pulse with tension, release, and intent. Now based in Berlin, Olivia continues to explore the intersections of activism, art, and sound – smashing the patriarchy, one beat at a time. Photography: Christophe Rassat

OMOLOKO is part of the new wave of artists who are leading the Brazilian
scene from the front. With 11 years of career, OMOLOKO has built a respected reputation for musical
authenticit y on the global stage. His international tours across Europe, Asia, Australia
and Latin America have taken him to demanding dance floors including Panorama Bar, Dekmantel, Circoloco, Adonis, Tomorrowland, Time Warp, Rober t Johnson, Primavera Sound, Wonder fruit Festival in Thailand and Heim Club in Shanghai. His work also extends to private events for brands such as Bottega Veneta, Soho House and Potato Head Bali. Founder and resident of the CurraLRec and Mathosa par ties in Belo Horizonte, he has lived in several regions of Brazil, shaping a sound that blends influences and emotions collected along his journey through the country. Rooted in the Brazilian underground, he fosters space for the LGBTQIAPN+ communit y, and his career reflects a path grounded in purpose, where music is always the main character. Photographer: Ivan Nishitani

wholefestival_description: A shining star born from the fusion of drag artistry and the DJ world. Their performances and DJ sets are captivating, full of raw and spicy energy, moving the crowd with every beat\ There are 2 states within them, represented as the Bung_10n party and :Dismal. Bung_10n is a lively, upbeat party for the LGBTQ+ community in Saigon, while :Dismal is a quieter, deeper side of them. But for both, when you show up, you wont be able to stop dancing. In Paris's DJ sets, They always aim to create a lively atmosphere with intense beats that keep people moving. My sets are a wild mix of Latin, Techno, Trance, Drum, Jersey Club, UK, Bailefunk, Donk, Hardstyle, and more. You'll get a sense of their sound after watching my Boiler Room set. Photographer: Chun Chin

Phoenix. The Dancing delinquent serving up chaos and poetry under the flashing lights of the Berlin rave scene. Known for their theatrical, sensual and romantic performances, and their soft punk attitude!

Poison Amrita is a sex worker and performer. Her work is unapologetically erotic and hyper feminine. She is inspired by vintage porn, punk, goth, and vintage fashion. She defines herself as an alternative showgirl stuck in between underground and pop culture. Half whore/half witch, she brings a sexy, badass, and mysterious energy on stage!

After last year's thrilling pole performance, Polefluid is bringing the energy down to earth. As a SWEAT regular, they’re known for their gogo dancing skills, and this year they’re taking it to the beach stage, baby! Expect a fusion of 80s aerobics, sensual masculinity, and a bold blend of gender fuckery. Photographer: Aime Dabbadie

Born in Brazil, Berlin-based and exported worldwide, PORNCEPTUAL is more than a party. It is a platform for forward thinking nightlife. What began as an intimate gathering has grown into an international presence contributing to contemporary club experiences. Blending queer club energy, sexual freedom, and uncompromising electronic sound, PORNCEPTUAL has built a global community that moves between dance floors across cities. As a music label, PRNCPTL showcases artists who challenge norms and expand sonic identities. Its releases spotlight forward thinking producers rooted in electronic music, experimental club, and hybrid forms, carrying the spirit of the dance floor into a lasting musical catalogue. Fearless and pleasure driven, PORNCEPTUAL exists at the intersection of sound, art, bodies, and freedom. It is a living ecosystem redefining contemporary club culture on an international scale.
soundcloud_description:
PRNCPTL: the music label from Pornceptual. 

With a commitment to diverse and experimental artistry, PRNCPTL is a platform for established and emerging talents across genres.

wholefestival_description: puppy, a Polish native, co-founder of Cuddles and a quadruple Scorpio nerd on a pursuit of hedonism, dopamine, and fun - always served with a touch of sarcasm and a punk edge. As one-half of Cuddles, the queer collective they co-founded with their partner Aaron Blau, puppy is creating a community and love-driven delusional playground where like-minded divas embrace, indulge, heal and unite. Born from Eastern European grit and shaped by Berlin’s underground and a soft spot for the unconventional, puppy’s trying to constantly reinvent the norm. Their sound is and always will be somewhere in between. Moving through drum work, house vocals, rough techno kicks and breaks to bassy, syncopated contemporary experimental, they're always playing with texture and rhythm and disobeying the definitions of genres. From deep, hypnotic moments to fierce, percussive peaks, they challenge conventions and embrace the chaos that shaped them. They have brought their energy to collectives and parties such as Gegen, Vitamina, Fairies, Group Therapy and many others, and are most often found across institutions including Æden, OXI, Fitzroy, KitKat,, Jolene, and Schwuz. Underneath the sharp humor and punk sensibility lies a soft, loving core which powers the commitment to music, community, and unapologetic self-expression. Puppy is a combination of the queer, the rebellious and the nonconformist that truly believes in not following the crowd blindly and instead being your own, strange self. with love, pup Photographer: Thomás Eyzaguierre

wholefestival_description: Puticlub is a sex-positive safer space for queer and trans people of color, the Latinx diaspora, and its allies. Together and beyond the exoticized gaze, Puticlub celebrates Latinx culture and music while challenging androcentrism.

From Bogotá, Colombia to the world. PvssyDivx is an international DJ based in Berlin and resident of Puticlub Berlin. Her sets are geographies of desire: perreo that pulses, Latin rhythms that don't ask for permission. She makes bodies sweat and asses move. Photographer: Meret Freisen

wholefestival_description: Behind the decks, Quelza moves with instinct and control, without ever losing the spark. His sets shift between textures and tempos without warning, bending genres and expectations in striking ways. Built on tension, release and a restless sense of movement, Quelza plays with emotion and anticipation, setting up moments that catch the crowds off guard and hit deeper than they expect. He is known for stretching time, guiding the floor through that in-between state where night slowly turns into morning, and no one wants it to end. Photographer: Sven Marquadt

Rakans is a Middle Eastern DJ and producer shaping East Germany’s underground between Berlin and Dresden. Blending hypnotic electronic sound with Middle Eastern textures, their sets channel intensity, emotion, and freedom. Founder of Rawmantique and Pornceptual resident, Rakans crafts dance floors where radical inclusivity and self-expression thrive.

wholefestival_description:
Roza Terenzi is a manifestation of femmeforward rhythmic energy, spreading a
concoction of sublime mind dance, irreverent sapphic mischief, & vivacious
cybernetics throughout the universe. As a producer, her music flirts with similarly enticing unknowns - zooming through
spheres of her own elegant velocity, dissolving constraints of time & dimension as
she fuses enchanted realms of spellbinding breakbeat science, introspective futch
electronica, & devilishly tranced out enchantress anthems. Throughout beloved
solo EPs on Kalahari Oyster Cult, Butter Sessions, & Maricas, & her full length
“Modern Bliss” LP on Planet Euphorique, she demonstrates an effortlessly eclectic
refusal to be confined to any one genre or tempo - blowing convention a kiss
goodbye as she ultimately lets her audacious spirit shine through in all that she
creates. Her fiercely universal approach also extends into a deep love of
collaboration essential to her practice, exemplified through EPs as B'Zircon with
Fantastic Man, Current Obsession with Furious Frank, & the much acclaimed “Edge
of Innocence” full length LP with D. Tiffany on Delicate Records. Photographer: Neelam Khan Vela

wholefestival_description: Born in Buenos Aires to Cape Verdean families, Rumi’s musical identity reflects a lifelong dialogue between his roots and the dancefloor — where the jazz, Brazilian, and Afro-Latin sounds that shaped his upbringing meet with his deep love and commitment to house, disco, and techno. What started as a hobby in 2005, took a serious turn upon his move to Vienna in 2016. Since then, he has become a driving force in the city’s underground scene. He is one of the founding members of A party called Jack , an event series and collective celebrating house music and its origins in funk, soul, and disco. Rumi later also launched GAZE , a platform amplifying the “queer gaze” on electronic music, visual arts, and performance — now a cornerstone of Vienna’s LGBTQI+ community. Rumi’s dynamic approach behind the decks (mixing both vinyl and digital) has led to appearances at internationally acclaimed institutions such as CLUB RAUM in Amsterdam, Panorama Bar in Berlin, and celebrated queer events including IsBurning, Horse Meat Disco Berlin, Gayhaze and Multisex. Rumi’s versatility also becomes apparent in the multiple B2B sessions he has held with many reknown local DJs, as well as with international artists like Carlos Valdes and Eris Drew. While he is currently working on his first release, Rumi’s musical identity can be found in online mixes for platforms such as Permanent Vacation, IsBurning, Refuge Worldwide, Kiosk Radio, and Vienna’s RES Radio – each a testament to his ongoing exploration of sound: from abstract ambient soundscapes and soulful rhythms to high-octane dance floor energy. Photographer: @luchiamrtnl

wholefestival_description: Miguel Santiago AKA Saint Guel is a DJ and producer based in Manila, Philippines. He has released music on Transit Records (PH), Sounds Nais (PH), FuFu Records (HK), and More Rice Records (TH), with upcoming releases on Equation (VN) and Philoxenia (DE). A curator and creator to the core, he channels his R&B, Soul, Jazz, Funk, and Disco roots into groovy, swanky house productions and warm, evocative DJ sets, shaped by years of playing across the Philippines and internationally. He is a resident at Wonderfruit Festival in Thailand and Equation Festival in Vietnam. Miguel is magnetic in and out of the booth, with an innate ability to connect on an intimate level — a quality that carries through in his sets, which feel deeply welcoming, like Filipino warmth translated into sound and the uncanny feeling of being right at home.

wholefestival_description: Salber Lee Williams is a Zimbabwean-Portuguese poet and performance artist, and founder of This Is Joy Mvmt—a movement making joy the point. Based in Berlin, her work explores joy as a radical practice of presence, resistance, and connection. Photographer: Valeria Mitelman

Cesar Gacitúa, a.k.a. Sara, is a Chilean DJ who burst onto the electronic music scene in 2019 with a sound that blends techno, hypnotic, and trance. Known for creating hypnotic atmospheres and high-energy sets, Sara transforms the dance floor into a continuous sonic journey. A resident DJ at Fiesta Dame, he has performed at the most influential parties in Santiago and throughout Chile.
soundcloud_description: Insta : sara.deejay

wholefestival_description: Sara Dziri is a Belgian-Tunisian electronic artist and DJ based in Brussels. Her sets move between hypnotic atmospheres and explosive club moments, blending trance-leaning rhythms, rolling percussion and sharp transitions into powerful dancefloor journeys. She is the co-founder of the Brussels platform Not Your Techno, which has helped shape radically inclusive dancefloors since 2019. A former long-time resident at Fuse, Dziri released her debut album on Optimo Music in 2022 and is now entering a new phase marked by new releases and an expanding presence on international dancefloors. Photographer: Louis Kerckhof

Schacke (they/them) is an artist, DJ, producer and label owner based in Copenhagen. The enigmatic Schacke defies categorization. Half Danish, half South Korean, they have been crafting music since their early teens, traversing genres and instruments with fearless curiosity. Starting with drums and emerging from roots in Metal, Noise, and Industrial, Schacke’s journey has been one of constant evolution. After moving to Copenhagen in 2013, Schacke quickly became a central figure in the city’s underground music scene. They co-ran the Mayhem venue and founded a DIY label for noise and industrial music. In 2015, Schacke joined the founding ranks of the Fast Forward collective, creating space for a new wave of young artists often overlooked by the city’s established techno scene. What began as a series of self-organized parties grew into a movement that helped shape the “Copenhagen Techno Sound”. Schacke’s discography spans releases on labels such as Kulør, ONES and ZEROS, Mama Told Ya, KAOS, Instruments of Discipline, and Intrepid Skin. In 2022, Schacke launched Sacred Interface, a label dedicated to pushing the boundaries of techno and trance while supporting local artists. Beyond the studio, they are a resident DJ, event organizer, and podcast runner for Bedside Productions, and in 2024 became a resident DJ for the Korgy Party in Helsinki. Schacke’s artistic philosophy is rooted in contrast and duality. Their music reflects a balance of light and shadow, drawing on an eclectic range of genres to create hypnotic rhythms, euphoric melodies, and evocative textures. On the dancefloor, Schacke channels the raver they once were, translating moments of euphoria and connection into their work. Photograoher: FEYD

wholefestival_description: Selma is a versatile artist who is drawn to many genres and brings her audience for a ride spanning rolling house grooves, percussive trips, trance cuts, euphoric vocal bombs and - if you’re lucky - a sprinkle of disco. She is currently based in Oslo, but has been strongly shaped by Copenhagen’s club community where she maintains a solid base as half of the DJ-duo Carlo & Selma and resident of club Den Anden Side. She is a co-founder of Group Therapy, a safer-space oriented party concept that centers queerness, diversity and community, while upholding that dancefloors are - and always have been - political. Photographer: Sarah Brik

wholefestival_description: Founded just over a year and a half ago in London by Umberto Borraccia and Timothy Twang, Sextou is a queer party and cultural platform rooted in techno, radical self-expression, and community. Running every six weeks on Fridays, Sextou takes its name from Brazilian slang meaning “Happy Friday.” The project has quickly established itself as a distinctive voice within London’s techno underground, known for its raw, immersive atmosphere and strong visual identity. More than just a party, Sextou is a space for queer liberation on the dancefloor — celebrating freedom, connection, and unapologetic expression through sound, bodies, and collective energy.

Shadysnack (she/her) is a Black transfeminine DJ based in Berlin. Her joy and passion for House music shines through her sets, where she blends funky tunes with Disco and House to create a joyful and loving atmosphere. Photographer: Frauke Joana

wholefestival_description: SHERELLE is the champion of high speed dance music who has led a new generation of club kids to tempos of 160bpm and beyond. Having toured the world twice over and ticked off the Professional DJ bucket list in the blink of an eye, SHERELLE is entering a new era of artistry with the release of her debut album With A Vengeance. With A Vengeance is the sound of uptempo catharsis, of SHERELLE making sense of a turbulent and difficult moment in time in her personal life. From intense daily writing sessions comes a 10-track LP that is powered by fierce feminine energy and reflects the hard/soft and light/dark dualities of the dancefloor and life itself. It is the cerebral joy of overcoming adversity. SHERELLE continues to wear her love of the underground on her sleeve with SHERELLELAND, the recession-busting club night that she’s been touring and which features unannounced line-ups and £10 tickets for her army of “bocats on a budget”. Behind the scenes, SHERELLE’s BEAUTIFUL project continues to empower Black and LGBTQI artists, with over 120 musicians having had access to the BEAUTIFUL studio in London, as per SHERELLE’s continued commitment to ownership and grassroots community in the music industry. Photographer: Sarah Louise Bennett

wholefestival_description: Intention circles are a way to reflect on why we party, how we party and how we can reintegrate the party into our everyday lives. Each participant is guided through individual reflection, setting intentions for the upcoming party, and (if they feel comfortable) sharing with the group. The aim is to encourage a collective ownership of the dance floor, and advocate for a harm reductive approach to partying.

Smoothie Boiz (he/him) is a rising star in Saigon’s queer underground music scene. As the heartbeat of @bung_l0n, one of Saigon’s most vibrant queer events, he brings an electrifying energy that embodies freedom, self-expression, and community. His sound blends playful melodies and infectious grooves with his unique style, capturing the spirit of queer culture and opening hearts on the dancefloor. With a passion for pushing boundaries, Smoothie Boiz is shaping a new wave of Vietnam’s electronic music scene: bold, colorful, and freedom. Having played for Boiler Room, Eastern Margins, Seoul Community Radio, Divine Heem (KR), and Church (PH). He continues to carve his unique path across Asia. Photographer: Nguyễn Anh Hào

Snax is an American-born electronic musician, DJ and performer based in Berlin. His artistic practice spans disco, house, funk and pop, informed by a background ranging from punk to club culture. Snax was 1/2 of disco band Captain Comatose, has released several solo albums, and is now collaborating with Panorama Bar resident nd_Baumecker as Snecker. At the Whole festival, Snax is thrilled to be providing keyboard madness on top of Massimiliano Pagliara’s DJ set. Photographer: Jason Harrell
soundcloud_description:
Singer, songwriter and keyboard whiz Snax, aka Paul Bonomo, began a life of performance in the suburbs of Washington DC, moving on to San Francisco, New York and now Berlin. Along the way, he founded the gay art rock band Fagbash, and was one half of disco-pop band Captain Comatose.
Soon after arriving in Berlin, Snax began producing his solo releases from the ground up, writing and performing mostly all instruments and vocals himself. In 2004 came the release of the electronic soul manifesto From The Rocking Chair To The Stage (Mental Groove). Fall 2006 saw Snax sound incorporating more acoustic elements on his second long-player Love Pollution (TNT/Four Music), described by Snax as being “about love, loss, bitchiness and finally redemption.” The remix for the single, ‘Honeymoon’s Over’ by Konrad Black was hailed by many as the anthem of Summer 2007. Returning to his electronic funk roots, Snax released two EP’s on UK’s defDrive followed by Special Guest Star, the flagship release on his own label, Random Records. Reflecting on his rich and rewarding career and the myriad creative interactions he’s experienced, Snax founded the House Of Snaxine. To them he dedicated Up And Coming Children (Random Records) in 2010; an exciting return to form with an eye to the future. 
Snax’s performances and DJ sets deliver provocation, sleaze and fun. The result is a direct connection with the audience. His various collaborators  have included Jamie Lidell, Peaches, Joey Arias and Terranova to name a few. Upon supporting The Scissor Sisters throughout Europe as their opening act, Snax was described be front man Jake Shears as “a one man fro freak show not to be missed.”
Currently, Snax is completing his fourth album, running his label and establishing his house music alter ego, Box Office Poison.

wholefestival_description: Spikey Lee grew up in the 90s when house & techno music thrived. His parents' love for dance music made him fall in love with the energy of house and techno at a young age. Fast forward 20-something years later, and Spikey Lee is travelling Europe to spread his love & knowledge of great music. He fills the room with a combination of early 90’s techno, sexy hard house, Detroit bangers, booty house, gabber and some trippy energetic beats.  When Spikey Lee is behind the decks, his music tells a story that keeps you guessing what’s next and never, never lets you stop dancing.

Known as the Godmother of House Music, Stacey “Hotwaxx” Hale is Detroit’s first female house music DJ and a pioneering force in the city’s electronic music history. For decades, she has moved audiences worldwide with soulful, high-energy sets that blend house, techno, funk, hip hop, and Motown influences, while holding residencies, radio shows, and international bookings. Beyond performance, she is a respected educator, mentor, and cultural leader who has helped shape Detroit’s music community through teaching, festival curation, and advocacy for women in music. Photographer: Maries Staggat
soundcloud_description:
In 2023  Blkout Wall Festivals  artist  Muralmatics  created  Hotwaxx Mural
*********************************************************************

For as long as there is music and those who faithfully dance, there  will always be a need for the Godmother of House Music, DJ Stacey “Hotwaxx” Hale. Detroit’s 1st female of house music plays dance floor bangers and orchestral melodies with live music fusions. Hotwaxx Hale has made a massive impact in American music culture, whether it’s playing in famous theaters like the Apollo New York and the Fox Detroit, or legendary underground dance music clubs like  ‘Studio 54,’ and ‘The Warehouse. Currently she holds residence at Spotlite Detroit, & Marble Bar Detroit.  She also has an international presence, which includes performances in London, Ibiza, France, Mexico, Berlin, Amsterdam, Toronto, and across the USA.   

 Hotwaxx Hale is the1st female DJ who played house music on the  radio in Detroit in the late 80s, and continues as a DJ to play a major role in curating the  soundtrack of our lives for several decades. Thriving in the city that started techno, she  somehow maintains a perfect flow by tastefully blending house and techno with funk,  hip hop and Motown soul, which is why she continues to have a diverse, multi-aged  devoted following.  Not only has she been resident DJ for several famous Motown radio stations, but she  also beat over 600 DJs in the Motor City Mix contest winning $10,000. She also has residents at two clubs in Detroit @ Spotlite Detroit as Housecats w/ Jenny LaFemme, and Panorama Berlin.  Hotwaxx  currently produces & hosts three radio shows. Twisted Tuesdays at 12n  Deepspaceradio.com EST. Every 3rd Friday a virtual show on Twitch & Mixcloud simultaneously thru Technoclub-net 4pm EST .  She is one of the leaders in a daily broadcast on Blast Radio/Hotwaxx.  Hotwaxx’s passion for music spills over into education for the youth and adults as well.  

Since the inception of  Girls Rock Detroit, Hotwaxx has been the DJ instructor helping young girls & non-binary find their way through mentorship and music.  
In 2023 she accepted the position as GRDs Co-Director for the next 2 years.

2024 Hotwaxx will led a virtual classroom for the Underground Music Academy (UMA)

 Hotwaxx is the Virtual & Hybrid Director Specialist for Streamteamio with various organizations worldwide.  Hotwaxx has also formed a live music group, Nyumba Muziki, which has performed at  the Charles H. Wright Museum of African American History, Detroit Opera House,  Detroit 300 Conservancy and more. 

Music Coordinator/DJ with  Jessica Care Moore’s Daughters of Betty (Black Women Rock Orchestra).   Hotwaxx has incredible production skills and has collaborated with artists like Eddie  Fowlkes, Alton Miller, Kevin Saunderson, Marissa Guzman, Norma Jean Bell,  Pirahnahead, K Hand, Diviniti Roxx, Candy J, Geri Free and Hazmat Live

Stacey is the creator and coordinator for  Sheometry Arts and Music Festival   launched in Detroit August 2019 returned in July 2024.  

Inducted into the  National  Museum of African American Music in 2019  Nashville TN.

Check out tracks on Bandcamp

The Godmother of House continues to  leave her mark and the best is yet to come.

SUEYA is a Black queer-led collective that centres Berlin's BIPOC queer community. Catch residents Karani and Caesarr on the Trina decks tearing through breaks, housey edits, RnB anthems and uptempo riddims. Come curious, come correct, come and shake sum!
soundcloud_description:
Berlin's Black queer led collective SUEYA centres its community, platforming DJs who move between the margins and the main room. Expect alternative events, global club sounds, and a space built by and for the people in it. 

Respect the culture. Pull up, tap in.

wholefestival_description: SWEAT is a place for queer and trans joy. We’re a Berlin-based DJ collective and club night hosting parties that offer a playful, intentional space to explore, connect, and celebrate. Expect energetic, bubbly, groovy sounds and performances from the DJs and artists from our community - it’s all about the dancefloor healing!

SXCL is a versatile musician from Reunion Island, producing and DJing since 2017 while continuously reshaping their sound to reflect an ever-evolving, eclectic taste. Moving fluidly from house and progressive trance to electro and old-school techno, they have brought their genre-blurring energy to iconic venues like Säule and Tresor, as well as to Festival Electropicales alongside artists such as Jeff Mills and Danilo Plessow. Sensitive to the intricate textures of electronic music, SXCL crafts immersive sets that seamlessly merge house and techno, guiding audiences through carefully constructed journeys tinged with a subtle, lingering melancholy.\n\nPhoto by @mdaniels____

wholefestival_description: sāda and coloray present Blue AM, a hybrid live narrative that weaves together music from 4 am and blue echo. The project unfolds as an abstract pop story about two people traveling through life, moving through loss, yet slowly finding their way toward each other.sāda’s emotional vocal presence merges with coloray’s poetic electronics to create a live experience centered around connection across cultures, intimacy, and the fragile details of a life slipping by.

wholefestival_description: Heart, emotion and passion instead of pure functionality – these are Tama Sumo's DJ characteristics in a nutshell. She started out in 1993 at Drama bar in Berlin / Kreuzberg, one of the first bars to feature its own DJ at the weekend and an important hangout for the scene before the clubs. Playing New York style vocal house, she quickly became a regular at Café Moskau and, from 1994, at the legendary Club Globus / Tresor, where she held a long-standing residency. Since 2001, Tama Sumo has been closely connected to OstGut and later Panorama Bar & Berghain, where she developed a varied style ranging from house, disco and techno to Detroit electro and pop. She approaches music with intuition and heart, creating sets that move with the crowd and play with mood and atmosphere. In 2008, she released her first record with Prosumer on Ostgut Ton. She has since collaborated and remixed for artists including Mary Boyoi and The Knife, and alongside Lakuti released “An Ode to Audre” for Ostgut Ton’s anniversary compilation in 2021. Together with Lakuti, she has curated Panorama Bar nights since 2013 and co-founded the Your Love events, centred on community and the celebration of a rich musical tapestry. Photographer: Sven Marquardt

Tedesco is one of the most sought-after emerging talents in London’s underground scene. Originally from Venezuela, their sets blend Latin club sounds and reggaeton with techno, bass, breaks, electro and house, creating a high-energy and eclectic sonic identity. A resident of SEXTOU and HOWL, Tedesco has performed at some of London’s most iconic queer parties, including UNFOLD, Tech Couture, Boudica, Riposte and TM8. They have played at renowned venues such as FOLD, Fabric, Corsica Studios, Phonox, Venue MOT, The Cause, Electrowerkz, Colour Factory, Number90, UNIT58 and EGG. Beyond the booth, Tedesco is also the co-founder of 2cperrea, a London-based queer party celebrating the sounds of the Latinx diaspora. Since launching, the collective has expanded across Europe with takeovers in cities including Berlin, Amsterdam, Barcelona and Prague, establishing itself as a key platform within both the London and European underground scenes. This year sees them continuing to build up their momentum across the globe. Photographer: Evan Woods

wholefestival_description: tracey is a queer-led Berlin event series blending music, performance, and art into immersive nightlife experiences. Driven by collective expression and bold programming, tracey champions queer creativity, progressive dancefloor culture, and spaces where identity, pleasure, and experimentation can thrive. Founded by a collective of DJs, performers, and cultural producers, tracey creates environments where nightlife becomes a platform for artistic exchange, queer visibility, and community connection. Each edition brings together DJs, performers, hosts, and visual artists to build dynamic participatory worlds shaped by music, bodies, and shared energy.

wholefestival_description: Trindade is a multifaceted independent artist, working as a DJ, cultural coordinator, and event producer. His goal is to spread electronic music from the periphery wherever he goes, moving between funk, techno, club music, house, and dancehall. His art represents the periphery, where he enables and occupies spaces of diversity and brings with him the musical curatorship of the periphery around Brazil and the world. He recently completed his first tour in Europe, passing through Paris, Lisbon, Porto, Rotterdam, Berlin, and Amsterdam. In Brazil, he has performed in Salvador, Brasília, São Paulo, Maringá, Londrina, Sorocaba, Natal, Aracaju, Recife, Campinas, and Rio de Janeiro. In addition, he is the CEO and founder of TREVVO, a collective focused on the peripheral LGBTQIA+ population, which enables, articulates, and creates plural and representative spaces in the scene of São Paulo and other states. Photographer: Nathan Phillips
soundcloud_description: comercialdjtrindade@gmail.com

wholefestival_description: A co-founder of SEXTOU LONDON and a pillar of London’s underground queer scene, TWANG is no stranger to the dancefloor. With regular appearances at cult institutions such as FOLD, UNFOLD, Tech Couture, HTBX, KAOS, HOWL, MAKAAN, and RIPOSTE, he has carved out a distinct presence behind the decks at BODY MOVEMENTS in London. Beyond the UK, TWANG has appeared in Berlin at KitKatClub for Gegen, as well as in Beijing at Groundless, and was also part of the 10-year anniversary of Herrensauna in London at FOLD — further cementing his position within the global queer techno landscape. His sets dive deep into the outer edges of techno, fusing hypnotic propulsion with trance-laced atmospheres. Layering deep textures, driving grooves, and surging energy, TWANG creates immersive journeys that keep dancefloors locked in motion. Balancing raw intensity with precision, his sound moves fluidly between power and subtlety — expansive, physical, and deeply personal. Photographer: Fran Gomez de Villaboa

wholefestival_description: Valav is a Venezuelan-born DJ and producer based in Barcelona, shaping a distinct sonic language he defines as Psygroove, a fusion of deep - hypnotic techno and psychedelic groove architecture.\nA passionate advocate for inclusion and equality, ValaV’s mission extends beyond music, he is committed to cultivating high-energy spaces where freedom of expression and collective intensity coexist.\nHis 2025 EP Aner Dypsijós marked a defining step in his artistic evolution, earning support from artists such as Richie Hawtin, Kangding Ray, Dax J and Anetha, and further establishing his presence within the international electronic circuit. Photographer: @unpocomais

wholefestival_description: DJ and producer originally from La Plata, now based in Buenos Aires. Founder of 999 ciclo, one of the most influential parties in the Argentine electronic music scene today. She also founded her own record label, N4IN9N4IN, through which she strongly supports the country’s national scene, including releasing her own music on the label. She has toured across Europe, Asia, and Latin America, and is now preparing for her third European tour alongside Manu Calmet and the project “Botox Fatal,” known for their live sets. She brings a powerful presence to the dancefloor, in a constant state of evolution. She creates dark, energetic techno with underground roots and isn’t afraid to venture into experimental territory. With plenty of local material, her sets invite the crowd to embrace the challenge with their whole body and feel the intensity in every hit. Photographers: @tebo.__ and @valencuesta

wholefestival_description: VINVAR is a Georgian DJ based in Berlin and a resident artist of Pornceptual. She is also co-founder of fluid.vision, a queer collective creating inclusive rave experiences for LGBTQIA+, FLINTA, and BIPOC communities. Primarily a techno DJ, VINVAR crafts hypnotic, percussion-driven, and tribal sets, mixing oldskool and newskool techno. Her sets are diverse, unpredictable, and high-energy, designed to immerse listeners and get them fully lost in the dance. Outside of techno, she explores many other dance genres, keeping her practice dynamic while rooted in techno. Shaped by Tbilisi’s underground club scene, VINVAR sees dancing as rebellion and release, bringing intensity, freedom, and connection to every party. Photographer: LEKO

wholefestival_description: With Us is a queer collective based in Paris that has been exclusively booking trans and non-binary people for the past four years! Between Paris, London, Berlin, and Strasbourg, With Us organizes parties created by and for queer communities, offering community-centered spaces to its audience by booking both local and international artists! Photographer: Solisse

ADIRA is a queer Arabic-pop club night and collective. Founded in 2023 by Lebanese artist Hassandra and Syrian-German DJ xanax_attax, ADIRA is a self-organized club night series that challenges orientalist stereotypes and embraces the diversity of queer Arab* identities. Our mission is to revive the nostalgia of 80’s, 90’s, and early 00’s Arabic-pop music, paying homage to iconic divas like Nawal Al Zoghbi, Haifa Wehbe, and Assala, alongside the unforgettable one-hit wonders from Melody Hits.

ÂTON’s sets navigate between SWANA sounds and club beats from the Global South, blending Afro-Arabic traditions, organic percussion, and contemporary grooves.Rooted in a queer and diasporic approach, his music explores the dance floor as a political and sensitive space, where marginalized bodies reclaim their place, power, and desire. Through celebration, ÂTON honors the margins with a clear commitment to amplifying them beyond dominant frameworks, transforming each set into a moment of memory, resistance, and collective liberation.
